- .specstory/history files

Please code review the pull request.

Review line by line and explain to me the purpose of each change. Use file names and line numbers to reference the changes, but also sequentially number every observation so we can easily reference them.

In the line by line review, pay attention to:

- clarity of variable names
- Go lang idiomatic code
- code clarity and simplicity, readable over clever
- "why" comments, not "how" comments
- missing comments
- missing log output
- missing analytics tracking
- single function exit point where possible (immediate guard clauses are OK)
- verify the code has to exist, is actually needed, and is in use
- verify the code is DRY, and doesn't replicate the same or similar code
- for test cases, ensure a data-driven approach is being used rather than lots of repetitive test code

Format your line by line review like this example:

```
  cmd/remote.go

  Line 12 (removed): Removed unused import of pkg/service package
  - (1) Ôťů Good - Cleaning up unused imports is proper Go hygiene

  Lines 157-162 (modified): Changed from loading config directly to using RPC client
  // Old: config, err := service.LoadConfig(dir)
  // New: rpcClient, err := client.NewRPCClient(dir)
  - (2) Ôťů Good architectural decision - Now operates via daemon RPC instead of direct file access
  - (3) Ôťů Proper defer cleanup pattern for RPC client
  - (4) Ôťů Variable name rpcClient is clear and follows conventions

  Lines 164-189 (new): Added remote status check before disabling
  - (5) Ôťů Good UX - Detects current connection state to provide better feedback
  - (6) Ôťů Type assertions use the two-value form (ok pattern) for safety
  - (7) ÔÜá´ŞĆ Nested if statements become deeply indented (4 levels) - could be refactored for readability
  - (8) Ôťů Variable names wasConnected, oldURL are descriptive
  - (9) ÔŁô Missing error handling - if remote.status call fails, we continue anyway. Should we?

  ---
  pkg/remote/sync.go
  Lines 260-265 (modified): Success messages now come after RPC call
  - (10) Ôťů Comment "config is now saved" is helpful context
  - (11) ÔÜá´ŞĆ Misleading comment placement - comment says "config is now saved" but we can't be certain from this code's perspective (that happens in the daemon)
```

In addition to the line by line review, suggest the 2-5 most important improvements for this code review.

In addition to the most important improvements, let's also think about if any of these changes could benefit from new, updated or expanded unit tests. We focus our unit tests on complicated logic, and combinatorial scenarios, not on coverage or completeness for completeness sake. Better to not have a unit test than to have tests that are simplistic or tautological. We also don't test 3rd party library or language features, only our own code.

For any new test cases that were added, confirm the tests are:

- Not tautological - They would catch real bugs
- Well-designed - Each assertion accurately verifies specific logic and complexity in the code being tested
- Reliable - They won't be flaky, don't rely on external state, and don't catch false positives.
- Not repetitive - They test different scenarios, and use data-driven testing rather than repetitive test code.

   107Ôćĺ+    27Ôćĺ|-----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-------------|------------------------|

   109Ôćĺ+    29Ôćĺ| [Codex CLI](https://www.openai.com/codex/cli/)            | [codexcli](pkg/providers/codexcli/)     | JSONL       | `~/.codex/sessions/`   |
   110Ôćĺ+    30Ôćĺ| [Cursor CLI](https://cursor.com/cli)                      | [cursorcli](pkg/providers/cursorcli/)   | SQLite      | `~/.cursor/chats/`     |
   111Ôćĺ+    31Ôćĺ| [Droid CLI](https://factory.ai/product/cli)               | [droidcli](pkg/providers/droidcli/)     | JSONL       | `~/.factory/sessions/` |
   112Ôćĺ+    32Ôćĺ| [Gemini CLI](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-cli)            | [geminicli](pkg/providers/geminicli/)   | JSON        | `~/.gemini/tmp/`       |
   113Ôćĺ+    33Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ+    34Ôćĺ### Agent Provider SPI (Service Provider Interface)
   115Ôćĺ+    35Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ+    36ÔćĺThere is also an [Agent SPI (Service Provider Interface)](/pkg/spi/) that allows you to extend the SpecStory CLI with support for new agent providers. Creating a provider to support a new agent, using the Provider SPI is documented [here](./docs/PROVIDER-SPI.md). Pull requests are welcome!
   117Ôćĺ+    37Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ+    38Ôćĺ## Installation & Usage
   119Ôćĺ+    39Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ+    40ÔćĺFull end-user installation and usage instructions are in the [SpecStory CLI Documentation](https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents). Installation for developers is covered [here](#development).
   121Ôćĺ+    41Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ+    42Ôćĺ### Quickstart Usage
   123Ôćĺ+    43Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ+    44ÔćĺBasic usage: `specstory [flags]`
   125Ôćĺ+    45Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ+    46ÔćĺFor help:
   127Ôćĺ+    47Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ+    48Ôćĺ```zsh
   129Ôćĺ+    49Ôćĺspecstory help
   130Ôćĺ+    50Ôćĺ```
   131Ôćĺ+    51Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ+    52Ôćĺor
   133Ôćĺ+    53Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ+    54Ôćĺ```zsh
   135Ôćĺ+    55Ôćĺspecstory help <command>
   136Ôćĺ+    56Ôćĺ# e.g.
   137Ôćĺ+    57Ôćĺspecstory help run
   138Ôćĺ+    58Ôćĺ```
   139Ôćĺ+    59Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ+    60ÔćĺInteractive auto-save mode:
   141Ôćĺ+    61Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ+    62Ôćĺ```zsh
   143Ôćĺ+    63Ôćĺ# Defaults to Claude Code if no provider is specified.
   144Ôćĺ+    64Ôćĺspecstory run <provider>
   145Ôćĺ+    65Ôćĺ# e.g.
   146Ôćĺ+    66Ôćĺspecstory run codex
   147Ôćĺ+    67Ôćĺ```
   148Ôćĺ+    68Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ+    69ÔćĺSyncing all files for the current project:
   150Ôćĺ+    70Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ+    71Ôćĺ```zsh
   152Ôćĺ+    72Ôćĺ# Defaults to syncing for all providers if no provider is specified
   153Ôćĺ+    73Ôćĺspecstory sync <provider>
   154Ôćĺ+    74Ôćĺ```
   155Ôćĺ+    75Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ+    76ÔćĺWith a specific session UUID:
   157Ôćĺ+    77Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ+    78Ôćĺ```zsh
   159Ôćĺ+    79Ôćĺspecstory sync -s <session-uuid>
   160Ôćĺ+    80Ôćĺ```
   161Ôćĺ+    81Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ+    82Ôćĺ## Configuration File
   163Ôćĺ+    83Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ+    84ÔćĺSpecStory CLI supports configuration files in TOML format. Settings can be configured at two levels:
   165Ôćĺ+    85Ôćĺ
   166Ôćĺ+    86Ôćĺ1. **User-level config**: `~/.specstory/cli/config.toml` - applies to all projects
   167Ôćĺ+    87Ôćĺ2. **Project-level config**: `.specstory/cli/config.toml` - applies only to the current project
   168Ôćĺ+    88Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ+    89ÔćĺConfiguration is loaded with the following priority (highest to lowest):
   170Ôćĺ+    90Ôćĺ1. CLI flags
   171Ôćĺ+    91Ôćĺ2. Project-level config (`.specstory/cli/config.toml`)
   172Ôćĺ+    92Ôćĺ3. User-level config (`~/.specstory/cli/config.toml`)
   173Ôćĺ+    93Ôćĺ
   174Ôćĺ+    94Ôćĺ### Example Configuration
   175Ôćĺ+    95Ôćĺ
   176Ôćĺ+    96Ôćĺ```toml
   177Ôćĺ+    97Ôćĺ# SpecStory CLI Configuration
   178Ôćĺ+    98Ôćĺ#
   179Ôćĺ+    99Ôćĺ# Uncomment (remove the #) the line and edit any setting below to change the default behavior.
   180Ôćĺ+   100Ôćĺ# For more information, see: https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage
   181Ôćĺ+   101Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ+   102Ôćĺ[local_sync]
   183Ôćĺ+   103Ôćĺ# Write markdown files locally. (default: true)
   184Ôćĺ+   104Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --only-cloud-sync
   185Ôćĺ+   105Ôćĺ
   186Ôćĺ+   106Ôćĺ# Custom output directory for markdown files.
   187Ôćĺ+   107Ôćĺ# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)
   188Ôćĺ+   108Ôćĺ# output_dir = "~/.specstory/history" # equivalent to --output-dir "~/.specstory/history"
   189Ôćĺ+   109Ôćĺ
   190Ôćĺ+   110Ôćĺ# Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (default: false, UTC)
   191Ôćĺ+   111Ôćĺ# local_time_zone = true # equivalent to --local-time-zone
   192Ôćĺ+   112Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ+   113Ôćĺ[cloud_sync]
   194Ôćĺ+   114Ôćĺ# Sync session data to SpecStory Cloud. (default: true, when logged in to SpecStory Cloud)
   195Ôćĺ+   115Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-cloud-sync
   196Ôćĺ+   116Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺ+   117Ôćĺ[logging]
   198Ôćĺ+   118Ôćĺ# Custom output directory for debug data.
   199Ôćĺ+   119Ôćĺ# Default: ./.specstory/debug (relative to the project directory)
   200Ôćĺ+   120Ôćĺ# debug_dir = "~/.specstory/debug" # equivalent to --debug-dir "~/.specstory/debug"
   201Ôćĺ+   121Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ+   122Ôćĺ# Error/warn/info output to stdout (default: false)
   203Ôćĺ+   123Ôćĺ# console = true # equivalent to --console
   204Ôćĺ+   124Ôćĺ
   205Ôćĺ+   125Ôćĺ# Write logs to .specstory/debug/debug.log (default: false)
   206Ôćĺ+   126Ôćĺ# log = true # equivalent to --log
   207Ôćĺ+   127Ôćĺ
   208Ôćĺ+   128Ôćĺ# Debug-level output, requires console or log (default: false)
   209Ôćĺ+   129Ôćĺ# debug = true # equivalent to --debug
   210Ôćĺ+   130Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ+   131Ôćĺ# Suppress all non-error output (default: false)
   212Ôćĺ+   132Ôćĺ# silent = true # equivalent to --silence
   213Ôćĺ+   133Ôćĺ
   214Ôćĺ+   134Ôćĺ[version_check]
   215Ôćĺ+   135Ôćĺ# Check for new versions of the CLI on startup.
   216Ôćĺ+   136Ôćĺ# Default: true
   217Ôćĺ+   137Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-version-check
   218Ôćĺ+   138Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ+   139Ôćĺ[analytics]
   220Ôćĺ+   140Ôćĺ<<<<<<< HEAD
   221Ôćĺ+   141Ôćĺ# Send anonymous usage analytics (default: true)
   222Ôćĺ+   142Ôćĺenabled = true
   223Ôćĺ+   143Ôćĺ
   224Ôćĺ+   144Ôćĺ[telemetry]
   225Ôćĺ+   145Ôćĺ# Enable OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics (default: false, auto-enabled if endpoint is set)
   226Ôćĺ+   146Ôćĺenabled = true
   227Ôćĺ+   147Ôćĺ# OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
   228Ôćĺ+   148Ôćĺendpoint = "localhost:4317"
   229Ôćĺ+   149Ôćĺ# Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
   230Ôćĺ+   150Ôćĺservice_name = "specstory-cli"
   231Ôćĺ+   151Ôćĺ# Exclude user prompt text from telemetry spans for privacy (default: false)
   232Ôćĺ+   152Ôćĺno_prompts = false
   233Ôćĺ+   153Ôćĺ=======
   234Ôćĺ+   154Ôćĺ# Send anonymous product usage analytics to help improve SpecStory.
   235Ôćĺ+   155Ôćĺ# Default: true
   236Ôćĺ+   156Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-usage-analytics
   237Ôćĺ+   157Ôćĺ
   238Ôćĺ+   158Ôćĺ[providers]
   239Ôćĺ+   159Ôćĺ# Agent execution commands by provider (used by specstory run)
   240Ôćĺ+   160Ôćĺ# Pass custom flags (e.g. claude_cmd = "claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions")
   241Ôćĺ+   161Ôćĺ# Use of these is equivalent to -c "custom command"
   242Ôćĺ+   162Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ+   163Ôćĺ# Claude Code command
   244Ôćĺ+   164Ôćĺ# claude_cmd = "claude"
   245Ôćĺ+   165Ôćĺ
   246Ôćĺ+   166Ôćĺ# Codex CLI command
   247Ôćĺ+   167Ôćĺ# codex_cmd = "codex"
   248Ôćĺ+   168Ôćĺ
   249Ôćĺ+   169Ôćĺ# Cursor CLI command
   250Ôćĺ+   170Ôćĺ# cursor_cmd = "cursor-agent"
   251Ôćĺ+   171Ôćĺ
   252Ôćĺ+   172Ôćĺ# Droid CLI command
   253Ôćĺ+   173Ôćĺ# droid_cmd = "droid"
   254Ôćĺ+   174Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ+   175Ôćĺ# Gemini CLI command
   256Ôćĺ+   176Ôćĺ# gemini_cmd = "gemini"
   257Ôćĺ+   177Ôćĺ>>>>>>> dev
   258Ôćĺ+   178Ôćĺ```
   259Ôćĺ+   179Ôćĺ
   260Ôćĺ+   180Ôćĺ### Configuration Options
   261Ôćĺ+   181Ôćĺ
   262Ôćĺ+   182Ôćĺ<<<<<<< HEAD
   263Ôćĺ+   183Ôćĺ| Section | Option | Default | Description |
   264Ôćĺ+   184Ôćĺ|---------|--------|---------|-------------|
   265Ôćĺ+   185Ôćĺ| (root) | `output_dir` | `.specstory/history` | Custom output directory for markdown files |
   266Ôćĺ+   186Ôćĺ| `[version_check]` | `enabled` | `true` | Check for newer CLI versions on startup |
   267Ôćĺ+   187Ôćĺ| `[cloud_sync]` | `enabled` | `true` | Sync sessions to SpecStory Cloud |
   268Ôćĺ+   188Ôćĺ| `[local_sync]` | `enabled` | `true` | Write local markdown files |
   269Ôćĺ+   189Ôćĺ| `[logging]` | `console` | `false` | Output logs to stdout |
   270Ôćĺ+   190Ôćĺ| `[logging]` | `log` | `false` | Write logs to debug file |
   271Ôćĺ+   191Ôćĺ| `[logging]` | `debug` | `false` | Enable debug-level output |
   272Ôćĺ+   192Ôćĺ| `[logging]` | `silent` | `false` | Suppress non-error output |
   273Ôćĺ+   193Ôćĺ| `[analytics]` | `enabled` | `true` | Send anonymous usage analytics |
   274Ôćĺ+   194Ôćĺ| `[telemetry]` | `enabled` | `false`* | Enable OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics |
   275Ôćĺ+   195Ôćĺ| `[telemetry]` | `endpoint` | `""` | OTLP gRPC collector endpoint |
   276Ôćĺ+   196Ôćĺ| `[telemetry]` | `service_name` | `"specstory-cli"` | Service name for telemetry |
   277Ôćĺ+   197Ôćĺ| `[telemetry]` | `no_prompts` | `false` | Exclude prompt text from telemetry spans |
   278Ôćĺ+   198Ôćĺ
   279Ôćĺ+   199Ôćĺ\* Telemetry is automatically enabled when an endpoint is configured, even if `enabled` is not explicitly set.
   280Ôćĺ+   200Ôćĺ=======
   281Ôćĺ+   201Ôćĺ| Section           | Option            | Default              | Description                                |
   282Ôćĺ+   202Ôćĺ|-------------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|
   283Ôćĺ+   203Ôćĺ| `[local_sync]`    | `enabled`         | `true`               | Write local markdown files                 |
   284Ôćĺ+   204Ôćĺ| `[local_sync]`    | `output_dir`      | `.specstory/history` | Custom output directory for markdown files |
   285Ôćĺ+   205Ôćĺ| `[local_sync]`    | `local_time_zone` | `false`              | Use local timezone for timestamps          |
   286Ôćĺ+   206Ôćĺ| `[cloud_sync]`    | `enabled`         | `true`               | Sync sessions to SpecStory Cloud           |
   287Ôćĺ+   207Ôćĺ| `[logging]`       | `debug_dir`       | `.specstory/debug`   | Custom output directory for debug data     |
   288Ôćĺ+   208Ôćĺ| `[logging]`       | `console`         | `false`              | Output logs to stdout                      |
   289Ôćĺ+   209Ôćĺ| `[logging]`       | `log`             | `false`              | Write logs to debug file                   |
   290Ôćĺ+   210Ôćĺ| `[logging]`       | `debug`           | `false`              | Enable debug-level output                  |
   291Ôćĺ+   211Ôćĺ| `[logging]`       | `silent`          | `false`              | Suppress non-error output                  |
   292Ôćĺ+   212Ôćĺ| `[version_check]` | `enabled`         | `true`               | Check for newer CLI versions on startup    |
   293Ôćĺ+   213Ôćĺ| `[analytics]`     | `enabled`         | `true`               | Send anonymous usage analytics             |
   294Ôćĺ+   214Ôćĺ| `[providers]`     | `claude_cmd`      | `"claude"`           | Claude Code command                        |
   295Ôćĺ+   215Ôćĺ| `[providers]`     | `codex_cmd`       | `"codex"`            | Codex CLI command                          |
   296Ôćĺ+   216Ôćĺ| `[providers]`     | `cursor_cmd`      | `"cursor-agent"`     | Cursor CLI command                         |
   297Ôćĺ+   217Ôćĺ| `[providers]`     | `droid_cmd`       | `"droid"`            | Droid CLI command                          |

   300Ôćĺ+   220Ôćĺ
   301Ôćĺ+   221Ôćĺ## Development
   302Ôćĺ+   222Ôćĺ
   303Ôćĺ+   223Ôćĺ### Development Prerequisites
   304Ôćĺ+   224Ôćĺ
   305Ôćĺ+   225Ôćĺ- macOS development environment
   306Ôćĺ+   226Ôćĺ- Go 1.25.1 or later
   307Ôćĺ+   227Ôćĺ- golangci-lint, latest version
   308Ôćĺ+   228Ôćĺ- Access to one or more terminal coding agents (e.g. Claude Code, Codex CLI, etc.)
   309Ôćĺ+   229Ôćĺ
   310Ôćĺ+   230ÔćĺYou'll want [Homebrew](https://brew.sh/) installed on your macOS system. Then:
   311Ôćĺ+   231Ôćĺ
   312Ôćĺ+   232Ôćĺ```zsh
   313Ôćĺ+   233Ôćĺbrew install go golangci-lint
   314Ôćĺ+   234Ôćĺ```
   315Ôćĺ+   235Ôćĺ
   316Ôćĺ+   236ÔćĺYou'll also want this test helper:
   317Ôćĺ+   237Ôćĺ
   318Ôćĺ+   238Ôćĺ```zsh
   319Ôćĺ+   239Ôćĺgo install gotest.tools/gotestsum@latest
   320Ôćĺ+   240Ôćĺ```
   321Ôćĺ+   241Ôćĺ
   322Ôćĺ+   242Ôćĺ### Building from source
   323Ôćĺ+   243Ôćĺ
   324Ôćĺ+   244Ôćĺ```zsh
   325Ôćĺ+   245Ôćĺ# Clone the repository
   326Ôćĺ+   246Ôćĺgit clone https://github.com/specstoryai/specstory-cli.git
   327Ôćĺ+   247Ôćĺ
   328Ôćĺ+   248Ôćĺ# Navigate to the project directory
   329Ôćĺ+   249Ôćĺcd specstory-cli
   330Ôćĺ+   250Ôćĺ
   331Ôćĺ+   251Ôćĺ# Build the project
   332Ôćĺ+   252Ôćĺgo build -o specstory
   333Ôćĺ+   253Ôćĺ```
   334Ôćĺ+   254Ôćĺ
   335Ôćĺ+   255ÔćĺYou can then run the built executable from there.
   336Ôćĺ+   256Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ+   257Ôćĺ### Check for Outdated Dependencies
   338Ôćĺ+   258Ôćĺ
   339Ôćĺ+   259Ôćĺ```zsh
   340Ôćĺ+   260Ôćĺgo list -m -u all
   341Ôćĺ+   261Ôćĺ```
   342Ôćĺ+   262Ôćĺ
   343Ôćĺ+   263Ôćĺ### Debug Raw Mode
   344Ôćĺ+   264Ôćĺ
   345Ôćĺ+   265ÔćĺThe `--debug-raw` flag enables a debug mode that is useful for developers working on the SpecStory CLI. It outputs the raw data from AI coding agents in a pretty-printed format. This hidden flag works with all operation modes and supports all providers (Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Droid CLI).
   346Ôćĺ+   266Ôćĺ
   347Ôćĺ+   267ÔćĺWhen enabled, it creates a debug directory structure under `.specstory/debug/` with individual pretty-printed JSON files for each record in the session as well as a JSON version of the SessionData returned from the provider for that session.
   348Ôćĺ+   268Ôćĺ
   349Ôćĺ+   269ÔćĺThis mode is useful for:
   350Ôćĺ+   270Ôćĺ- Understanding the raw data structure from different AI coding agents
   351Ôćĺ+   271Ôćĺ- Analyzing conversation flow and metadata
   352Ôćĺ+   272Ôćĺ- Debugging parsing issues
   353Ôćĺ+   273Ôćĺ- Troubleshooting agent-specific data formats
   354Ôćĺ+   274Ôćĺ
   355Ôćĺ+   275ÔćĺRun mode with debug output:
   356Ôćĺ+   276Ôćĺ
   357Ôćĺ+   277Ôćĺ```zsh
   358Ôćĺ+   278Ôćĺ./specstory run --debug-raw
   359Ôćĺ+   279Ôćĺ```
   360Ôćĺ+   280Ôćĺ
   361Ôćĺ+   281ÔćĺSync mode with debug output:
   362Ôćĺ+   282Ôćĺ
   363Ôćĺ+   283Ôćĺ```zsh
   364Ôćĺ+   284Ôćĺ./specstory sync --debug-raw
   365Ôćĺ+   285Ôćĺ```
   366Ôćĺ+   286Ôćĺ
   367Ôćĺ+   287ÔćĺSync specific session with debug output:
   368Ôćĺ+   288Ôćĺ
   369Ôćĺ+   289Ôćĺ```zsh
   370Ôćĺ+   290Ôćĺ./specstory sync -s <session-id> --debug-raw
   371Ôćĺ+   291Ôćĺ```
   372Ôćĺ+   292Ôćĺ
   373Ôćĺ+   293Ôćĺ**Output Structure:**
   374Ôćĺ+   294Ôćĺ
   375Ôćĺ+   295Ôćĺ```
   376Ôćĺ+   296Ôćĺ.specstory/debug/
   377Ôćĺ+   297ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ <session-uuid>/
   378Ôćĺ+   298Ôćĺ    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 1.json      # Claude Code: sequential numbering
   379Ôćĺ+   299Ôćĺ    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 2.json      # Cursor CLI: based on rowid
   380Ôćĺ+   300Ôćĺ    ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 3.json
   381Ôćĺ+   301Ôćĺ    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ...
   382Ôćĺ+   302Ôćĺ    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ session-data.json # JSON version of the SessionData returned from the provider for this session
   383Ôćĺ+   303Ôćĺ```
   384Ôćĺ+   304Ôćĺ
   385Ôćĺ+   305ÔćĺEach JSON file is pretty-printed with 2-space indentation. For Claude Code, files are numbered sequentially based on their position in the JSONL file. For Cursor CLI, files are numbered based on the SQLite rowid.
   386Ôćĺ+   306Ôćĺ
   387Ôćĺ+   307Ôćĺ**Example:**
   388Ôćĺ+   308Ôćĺ
   389Ôćĺ+   309ÔćĺIf processing a session with ID `30cc3569-a9d4-429e-981a-ab73e3ddee5f`, the debug files will be created in: `.specstory/debug/30cc3569-a9d4-429e-981a-ab73e3ddee5f/`
   390Ôćĺ+   310Ôćĺ
   391Ôćĺ+   311Ôćĺ## Testing
   392Ôćĺ+   312Ôćĺ
   393Ôćĺ+   313ÔćĺTo run all tests with easy to read output:
   394Ôćĺ+   314Ôćĺ
   395Ôćĺ+   315Ôćĺ```zsh
   396Ôćĺ+   316Ôćĺgotestsum ./...
   397Ôćĺ+   317Ôćĺ```
   398Ôćĺ+   318Ôćĺ
   399Ôćĺ+   319ÔćĺRun tests with verbose output:
   400Ôćĺ+   320Ôćĺ
   401Ôćĺ+   321Ôćĺ```zsh
   402Ôćĺ+   322Ôćĺgo test -v ./...
   403Ôćĺ+   323Ôćĺ```
   404Ôćĺ+   324Ôćĺ
   405Ôćĺ+   325ÔćĺTest specific packages:
   406Ôćĺ+   326Ôćĺ
   407Ôćĺ+   327Ôćĺ```zsh
   408Ôćĺ+   328Ôćĺgo test -v ./pkg/cli 
   409Ôćĺ+   329Ôćĺ```
   410Ôćĺ+   330Ôćĺ
   411Ôćĺ+   331ÔćĺRun specific tests (e.g., filename generation tests)
   412Ôćĺ+   332Ôćĺ
   413Ôćĺ+   333Ôćĺ```zsh
   414Ôćĺ+   334Ôćĺgo test  -v ./pkg/cli -run TestGenerateFilenameFromUserMessage
   415Ôćĺ+   335Ôćĺ```
   416Ôćĺ+   336Ôćĺ
   417Ôćĺ+   337ÔćĺTesting specific features
   418Ôćĺ+   338Ôćĺ
   419Ôćĺ+   339Ôćĺ```zsh
   420Ôćĺ+   340Ôćĺ# Test the new filename generation logic
   421Ôćĺ+   341Ôćĺgo test -v ./pkg/cli -run "TestExtractWordsFromMessage|TestGenerateFilenameFromWords|TestGenerateFilenameFromUserMessage"
   422Ôćĺ+   342Ôćĺ```
   423Ôćĺ+   343Ôćĺ
   424Ôćĺ+   344Ôćĺ## Linting
   425Ôćĺ+   345Ôćĺ
   426Ôćĺ+   346ÔćĺThis project uses [golangci-lint](https://golangci-lint.run/) for code quality checks. The configuration enables all default linters plus `gofmt` and `goimports` for consistent formatting.
   427Ôćĺ+   347Ôćĺ
   428Ôćĺ+   348Ôćĺ### Running the linter
   429Ôćĺ+   349Ôćĺ
   430Ôćĺ+   350ÔćĺCheck all Go files in the project:
   431Ôćĺ+   351Ôćĺ
   432Ôćĺ+   352Ôćĺ```zsh
   433Ôćĺ+   353Ôćĺgolangci-lint run
   434Ôćĺ+   354Ôćĺ```
   435Ôćĺ+   355Ôćĺ
   436Ôćĺ+   356ÔćĺAutomatically fix issues where possible:
   437Ôćĺ+   357Ôćĺ
   438Ôćĺ+   358Ôćĺ```zsh
   439Ôćĺ+   359Ôćĺgolangci-lint run --fix
   440Ôćĺ+   360Ôćĺ```
   441Ôćĺ+   361Ôćĺ
   442Ôćĺ+   362ÔćĺCheck a specific package:
   443Ôćĺ+   363Ôćĺ
   444Ôćĺ+   364Ôćĺ```zsh
   445Ôćĺ+   365Ôćĺgolangci-lint run ./pkg/analytics/...
   446Ôćĺ+   366Ôćĺ```
   447Ôćĺ+   367Ôćĺ
   448Ôćĺ+   368Ôćĺ**Note:** Always run the linter on directories or packages, not individual files. Running on single files can cause false positives where symbols from other files in the same package cannot be resolved.
   449Ôćĺ+   369Ôćĺ
   450Ôćĺ+   370ÔćĺFormat code:
   451Ôćĺ+   371Ôćĺ
   452Ôćĺ+   372Ôćĺ```zsh
   453Ôćĺ+   373Ôćĺgofmt -w .
   454Ôćĺ+   374Ôćĺ```
   455Ôćĺ+   375Ôćĺ
   456Ôćĺ+   376Ôćĺ### Linter Configuration
   457Ôćĺ+   377Ôćĺ
   458Ôćĺ+   378ÔćĺThe linter configuration is in `.golangci.yml`. Key linters include:
   459Ôćĺ+   379Ôćĺ- **errcheck**: Ensures error return values are checked
   460Ôćĺ+   380Ôćĺ- **gofmt**: Enforces standard Go formatting
   461Ôćĺ+   381Ôćĺ- **goimports**: Manages import statements
   462Ôćĺ+   382Ôćĺ- **staticcheck**: Comprehensive bug detection
   463Ôćĺ+   383Ôćĺ- **govet**: Reports suspicious constructs
   464Ôćĺ+   384Ôćĺ
   465Ôćĺ+   385ÔćĺTo see all enabled linters:
   466Ôćĺ+   386Ôćĺ
   467Ôćĺ+   387Ôćĺ```zsh
   468Ôćĺ+   388Ôćĺgolangci-lint linters
   469Ôćĺ+   389Ôćĺ```
   470Ôćĺ+   390Ôćĺ
   471Ôćĺ+   391Ôćĺ## Analytics
   472Ôćĺ+   392Ôćĺ
   473Ôćĺ+   393ÔćĺSpecStory CLI collects anonymous usage analytics to PostHog to help improve the product. The following events are tracked:
   474Ôćĺ+   394Ôćĺ
   475Ôćĺ+   395Ôćĺ- Extension activation (in interacive mode) - ext_activated
   476Ôćĺ+   396Ôćĺ- Successful markdown sync operations - ext_sync_markdown_success
   477Ôćĺ+   397Ôćĺ- Failed markdown sync operations - ext_sync_markdown_error
   478Ôćĺ+   398Ôćĺ- First-time autosave of new sessions - ext_autosave_success
   479Ôćĺ+   399Ôćĺ- Failed first-time autosave of new sessions - ext_autosave_error
   480Ôćĺ+   400Ôćĺ
   481Ôćĺ+   401ÔćĺAll analytics are processed through PostHog with GeoIP enabled for general location data.
   482Ôćĺ+   402Ôćĺ
   483Ôćĺ+   403Ôćĺ### Disabling Analytics
   484Ôćĺ+   404Ôćĺ
   485Ôćĺ+   405ÔćĺTo opt out of analytics tracking, use the `--no-usage-analytics` flag:
   486Ôćĺ+   406Ôćĺ
   487Ôćĺ+   407Ôćĺ```bash
   488Ôćĺ+   408Ôćĺspecstory --no-usage-analytics [other options]
   489Ôćĺ+   409Ôćĺ```
   490Ôćĺ+   410Ôćĺ
   491Ôćĺ+   411Ôćĺ**Note**: Error tracking events include a 500ms delay before the program exits to ensure events are sent successfully. This is necessary because PostHog sends events asynchronously.
   492Ôćĺ+   412Ôćĺ
   493Ôćĺ+   413Ôćĺ### Development Analytics
   494Ôćĺ+   414Ôćĺ
   495Ôćĺ+   415ÔćĺAnalytics are disabled in development builds by default. To enable analytics during local development, build with the PostHog API key:
   496Ôćĺ+   416Ôćĺ
   497Ôćĺ+   417Ôćĺ```zsh
   498Ôćĺ+   418Ôćĺexport POSTHOG_API_KEY="your-posthog-api-key"
   499Ôćĺ+   419Ôćĺgo build -ldflags "-X github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics.apiKey=$POSTHOG_API_KEY" -o specstory ./
   500Ôćĺ+   420Ôćĺ```
```
</details></tool-use>

 30704Ôćĺ+## Analytics
 30705Ôćĺ+
 30706Ôćĺ+SpecStory CLI collects anonymous usage analytics to PostHog to help improve the product. The following events are tracked:
 30707Ôćĺ+
 30708Ôćĺ+- Extension activation (in interacive mode) - ext_activated
 30709Ôćĺ+- Successful markdown sync operations - ext_sync_markdown_success
 30710Ôćĺ+- Failed markdown sync operations - ext_sync_markdown_error
 30711Ôćĺ+- First-time autosave of new sessions - ext_autosave_success
 30712Ôćĺ+- Failed first-time autosave of new sessions - ext_autosave_error
 30713Ôćĺ+
 30714Ôćĺ+All analytics are processed through PostHog with GeoIP enabled for general location data.
 30715Ôćĺ+
 30716Ôćĺ+### Disabling Analytics
 30717Ôćĺ+
 30718Ôćĺ+To opt out of analytics tracking, use the `--no-usage-analytics` flag:
 30719Ôćĺ+
 30720Ôćĺ+```zsh
 30721Ôćĺ+specstory --no-usage-analytics [other options]
 30722Ôćĺ+```
 30723Ôćĺ+
 30724Ôćĺ+**Note**: Error tracking events include a 500ms delay before the program exits to ensure events are sent successfully. This is necessary because PostHog sends events asynchronously.
 30725Ôćĺ+
 30726Ôćĺ+### Development Analytics
 30727Ôćĺ+
 30728Ôćĺ+Analytics are disabled in development builds by default. To enable analytics during local development, build with the PostHog API key:
 30729Ôćĺ+
 30730Ôćĺ+```zsh
 30731Ôćĺ+export POSTHOG_API_KEY="your-posthog-api-key"
 30732Ôćĺ+go build -ldflags "-X github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics.apiKey=$POSTHOG_API_KEY" -o specstory ./
 30733Ôćĺ+```
 30734Ôćĺ+
 30735Ôćĺ+## Open Telemetry
 30736Ôćĺ+
 30737Ôćĺ+SpecStory CLI supports [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) Protocol (OTLP) tracing and metrics for observability. When enabled, it emits spans for session processing with detailed attributes about exchanges, messages, tool usage, and token consumption.
 30738Ôćĺ+
 30739Ôćĺ+### Enabling Telemetry
 30740Ôćĺ+
 30741Ôćĺ+Optional OpenTelemetry data can be enabled in three ways:
 30742Ôćĺ+
 30743Ôćĺ+1. **Standard OTEL Environment variables** (highest priority for telemetry settings):
 30744Ôćĺ+   
 30745Ôćĺ+   ```zsh
 30746Ôćĺ+   export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT="localhost:4317"
 30747Ôćĺ+   export OTEL_SERVICE_NAME="specstory-cli"
 30748Ôćĺ+   export OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES="user_id_hash=user_name,env=dev"
 30749Ôćĺ+   
 30750Ôćĺ+   specstory run
 30751Ôćĺ+   # or
 30752Ôćĺ+   specstory sync
 30753Ôćĺ+   # or
 30754Ôćĺ+   specstory watch
 30755Ôćĺ+   ```
 30756Ôćĺ+
 30757Ôćĺ+2. **CLI flags**:
 30758Ôćĺ+   
 30759Ôćĺ+   ```zsh
 30760Ôćĺ+   specstory sync --telemetry-endpoint localhost:4317 --telemetry-service-name my-service --no-telemetry-prompts
 30761Ôćĺ+   ```
 30762Ôćĺ+
 30763Ôćĺ+3. **Configuration files** (`./.specstory/cli/config.toml` or `~/.specstory/cli/config.toml`):
 30764Ôćĺ+
 30765Ôćĺ+   ```toml
 30766Ôćĺ+   [telemetry]
 30767Ôćĺ+   # OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
 30768Ôćĺ+   endpoint = "localhost:4317"
 30769Ôćĺ+
 30770Ôćĺ+   # Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
 30771Ôćĺ+   service_name = "my-service-name"
 30772Ôćĺ+
 30773Ôćĺ+   # Exclude user prompt text from telemetry spans for privacy (default: true)
 30774Ôćĺ+   prompts = false
 30775Ôćĺ+   ```
 30776Ôćĺ+
 30777Ôćĺ+### Telemetry Configuration Options
 30778Ôćĺ+
 30779Ôćĺ+| Option       | CLI Flag                   | Environment Variable          | Config Key               | Default         | Description                            |
 30780Ôćĺ+|--------------|----------------------------|-------------------------------|--------------------------|-----------------|----------------------------------------|
 30781Ôćĺ+| Disable      | default                    | `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED`           |                          |                 | Disable telemetry                      |
 30782Ôćĺ+| Endpoint     | `--telemetry-endpoint`     | `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` | `telemetry.endpoint`     | `""`            | OTLP gRPC collector endpoint           |
 30783Ôćĺ+| Service Name | `--telemetry-service-name` | `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`           | `telemetry.service_name` | `specstory-cli` | Service name for spans/metrics         |
 30784Ôćĺ+| No Prompts   | `--no-telemetry-prompts`   | -                             | `telemetry.prompts`      | `true`          | Include/exclude prompt text from spans |
 30785Ôćĺ+
 30786Ôćĺ+**Note**: Telemetry is enabled when an endpoint is configured unless the standard `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED` ENV var is set to `true` or `1`.
 30787Ôćĺ+
 30788Ôćĺ+### Excluding Prompt Text
 30789Ôćĺ+
 30790Ôćĺ+You can exclude user prompt text from telemetry spans. When excluded, the `specstory.exchange.prompt_text` attribute will be empty.
 30791Ôćĺ+
 30792Ôćĺ+Via CLI flag:
 30793Ôćĺ+
 30794Ôćĺ+```zsh
 30795Ôćĺ+specstory sync --no-telemetry-prompts
 30796Ôćĺ+```
 30797Ôćĺ+
 30798Ôćĺ+Via configuration files:
 30799Ôćĺ+
 30800Ôćĺ+```toml
 30801Ôćĺ+[telemetry]
 30802Ôćĺ+# Exclude user prompt text from telemetry spans for privacy (default: true)
 30803Ôćĺ+prompts = false
 30804Ôćĺ+```
 30805Ôćĺ+
 30806Ôćĺ+### Disabling Telemetry
 30807Ôćĺ+
 30808Ôćĺ+Telemetry is disabled unless you configure an OLTP endpoint. If you do want to disable telemetry while keeping an endpoint configured, you can use `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED`, which is a standard OTel convention:
 30809Ôćĺ+
 30810Ôćĺ+```zsh
 30811Ôćĺ+export OTEL_SDK_DISABLED="true"
 30812Ôćĺ+```
 30813Ôćĺ+
 30814Ôćĺ+### Telemetry Data
 30815Ôćĺ+
 30816Ôćĺ+#### Session Span Attributes
 30817Ôćĺ+
 30818Ôćĺ+Each session processing span includes the following attributes:
 30819Ôćĺ+
 30820Ôćĺ+| Attribute                                   | Description                                    |
 30821Ôćĺ+|---------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
 30822Ôćĺ+| `specstory.agent`                           | The agent provider name (e.g., "claude-code")  |
 30823Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.id`                      | Unique session identifier                      |
 30824Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.exchange_count`          | Number of exchanges in the session             |
 30825Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.message_count`           | Total messages across all exchanges            |
 30826Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tool_count`              | Total tool invocations                         |
 30827Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tool_type_count`         | Number of unique tool types used               |
 30828Ôćĺ+| `specstory.project.path`                    | Workspace root path                            |
 30829Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.input`            | Total input tokens (all providers)             |
 30830Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.output`           | Total output tokens (all providers)            |
 30831Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.cache_creation`   | Cache creation tokens (Claude Code, Droid CLI) |
 30832Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.cache_read`       | Cache read tokens (Claude Code, Droid CLI)     |
 30833Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.cached_input`     | Cached input tokens (Codex CLI)                |
 30834Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.reasoning_output` | Reasoning output tokens (Codex CLI)            |
 30835Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.cached`           | Cached tokens (Gemini CLI)                     |
 30836Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.thought`          | Thought/reasoning tokens (Gemini CLI)          |
 30837Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.tool`             | Tool-related tokens (Gemini CLI)               |
 30838Ôćĺ+| `specstory.session.tokens.thinking`         | Thinking tokens (Droid CLI)                    |
 30839Ôćĺ+
 30840Ôćĺ+#### Exchange Span Attributes
 30841Ôćĺ+
 30842Ôćĺ+Each exchange is recorded as a child span with these attributes:
 30843Ôćĺ+
 30844Ôćĺ+| Attribute                                    | Description                                    |
 30845Ôćĺ+|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
 30846Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.id`                      | Exchange identifier                            |
 30847Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.index`                   | Exchange index in session                      |
 30848Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.model`                   | Model used for this exchange                   |
 30849Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.prompt_text`             | User prompt text (unless `no_prompts` is set)  |
 30850Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.start_time`              | Exchange start timestamp                       |
 30851Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.end_time`                | Exchange end timestamp                         |
 30852Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.message_count`           | Messages in this exchange                      |
 30853Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tools_used`              | Comma-separated tool names                     |
 30854Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tool_types`              | Comma-separated tool types                     |
 30855Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tool_count`              | Number of tool invocations                     |
 30856Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.input`            | Input tokens for this exchange                 |
 30857Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.output`           | Output tokens for this exchange                |
 30858Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.cache_creation`   | Cache creation tokens (Claude Code, Droid CLI) |
 30859Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.cache_read`       | Cache read tokens (Claude Code, Droid CLI)     |
 30860Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.cached_input`     | Cached input tokens (Codex CLI)                |
 30861Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.reasoning_output` | Reasoning output tokens (Codex CLI)            |
 30862Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.cached`           | Cached tokens (Gemini CLI)                     |
 30863Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.thought`          | Thought/reasoning tokens (Gemini CLI)          |
 30864Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.tool`             | Tool-related tokens (Gemini CLI)               |
 30865Ôćĺ+| `specstory.exchange.tokens.thinking`         | Thinking tokens (Droid CLI)                    |
 30866Ôćĺ+

 30899Ôćĺ-```
 30900Ôćĺ-

 31841Ôćĺ+// extractUsageFromTokenCount extracts token usage from a token_count event's info.last_token_usage
 31842Ôćĺ+// Codex CLI emits token_count events with the structure:
 31843Ôćĺ+//
 31844Ôćĺ+//	{
 31845Ôćĺ+//	  "type": "event_msg",
 31846Ôćĺ+//	  "payload": {
 31847Ôćĺ+//	    "type": "token_count",
 31848Ôćĺ+//	    "info": {
 31849Ôćĺ+//	      "last_token_usage": { "input_tokens": N, "cached_input_tokens": N, "output_tokens": N, "reasoning_output_tokens": N, ... },
 31850Ôćĺ+//	      "total_token_usage": { ... }
 31851Ôćĺ+//	    }
 31852Ôćĺ+//	  }
 31853Ôćĺ+//	}
 31854Ôćĺ+//
 31855Ôćĺ+// We use last_token_usage for per-turn usage (total_token_usage is cumulative).
 31856Ôćĺ+// Codex CLI uses its own token field names, stored in the Codex-specific fields of Usage.
 31857Ôćĺ+func extractUsageFromTokenCount(payload map[string]interface{}) *Usage {
 31858Ôćĺ+	info, ok := payload["info"].(map[string]interface{})
 31859Ôćĺ+	if !ok {
 31860Ôćĺ+		return nil
 31861Ôćĺ+	}
 31862Ôćĺ+
 31863Ôćĺ+	// Use last_token_usage for per-turn metrics (not cumulative)
 31864Ôćĺ+	lastUsage, ok := info["last_token_usage"].(map[string]interface{})
 31865Ôćĺ+	if !ok {
 31866Ôćĺ+		return nil
 31867Ôćĺ+	}
 31868Ôćĺ+
 31869Ôćĺ+	return &Usage{
 31870Ôćĺ+		// Common fields
 31871Ôćĺ+		InputTokens:  schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "input_tokens"),
 31872Ôćĺ+		OutputTokens: schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "output_tokens"),
 31873Ôćĺ+		// Codex CLI specific fields
 31874Ôćĺ+		CachedInputTokens:     schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "cached_input_tokens"),
 31875Ôćĺ+		ReasoningOutputTokens: schema.GetIntFromMap(lastUsage, "reasoning_output_tokens"),
 31876Ôćĺ+	}
 31877Ôćĺ+}

 32176Ôćĺ+// settingsPathFromSession returns the path to the .settings.json file

 32178Ôćĺ+// Example: /path/to/session.jsonl -> /path/to/session.settings.json
 32179Ôćĺ+func settingsPathFromSession(sessionPath string) string {

 32185Ôćĺ+}

 32620Ôćĺ+// computeExchangeStats computes statistics for a single exchange.
 32621Ôćĺ+func computeExchangeStats(exchange schema.Exchange, idx int) ExchangeStats {
 32622Ôćĺ+	toolNames, toolTypes, toolCount := extractToolInfo(exchange)
 32623Ôćĺ+	tokenUsage := countExchangeTokens(exchange)
 32624Ôćĺ+
 32625Ôćĺ+	return ExchangeStats{
 32626Ôćĺ+		ExchangeID:   exchange.ExchangeID,
 32627Ôćĺ+		ExchangeIdx:  idx,
 32628Ôćĺ+		PromptText:   extractUserPromptText(exchange),
 32629Ôćĺ+		StartTime:    exchange.StartTime,
 32630Ôćĺ+		EndTime:      exchange.EndTime,
 32631Ôćĺ+		MessageCount: len(exchange.Messages),
 32632Ôćĺ+		ToolNames:    toolNames,

 32639Ôćĺ+
 32640Ôćĺ+// ComputeSessionStats computes all statistics for a session from its SessionData.
 32641Ôćĺ+func ComputeSessionStats(agentName string, session *spi.AgentChatSession) SessionStats {
 32642Ôćĺ+	stats := SessionStats{
 32643Ôćĺ+		AgentName: agentName,
 32644Ôćĺ+		SessionID: session.SessionID,
 32645Ôćĺ+	}
 32646Ôćĺ+
 32647Ôćĺ+	if session.SessionData != nil {
 32648Ôćĺ+		stats.ProjectPath = session.SessionData.WorkspaceRoot
 32649Ôćĺ+		stats.ExchangeCount = len(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
 32650Ôćĺ+		stats.MessageCount = countSessionMessages(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
 32651Ôćĺ+		stats.ToolCount, stats.ToolTypeCount = countSessionTools(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
 32652Ôćĺ+		stats.TokenUsage = countSessionTokens(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
 32653Ôćĺ+	}
 32654Ôćĺ+
 32655Ôćĺ+	return stats

 32731Ôćĺ+
 32732Ôćĺ+// ProcessExchangeSpans creates child spans for all exchanges in a session.
 32733Ôćĺ+// This is a helper that creates spans, sets attributes, and ends them immediately
 32734Ôćĺ+// since exchange processing is retrospective (data already exists).
 32735Ôćĺ+// When noPrompts is true, the prompt_text attribute will be empty for privacy.
 32736Ôćĺ+func ProcessExchangeSpans(ctx context.Context, stats SessionStats, exchanges []schema.Exchange, noPrompts bool) {
 32737Ôćĺ+	telemetryLogger().Debug("Creating exchange spans",
 32738Ôćĺ+		"sessionId", stats.SessionID,
 32739Ôćĺ+		"exchangeCount", len(exchanges),
 32740Ôćĺ+		"noPrompts", noPrompts,
 32741Ôćĺ+	)
 32742Ôćĺ+	for i, exchange := range exchanges {
 32743Ôćĺ+		_, exchangeSpan := startExchangeSpan(ctx, stats.SessionID, exchange.ExchangeID, i)
 32744Ôćĺ+		exchangeStats := computeExchangeStats(exchange, i)
 32745Ôćĺ+		if noPrompts {
 32746Ôćĺ+			exchangeStats.PromptText = ""
 32747Ôćĺ+		}
 32748Ôćĺ+		setExchangeSpanAttributes(exchangeSpan, exchangeStats)
 32749Ôćĺ+		exchangeSpan.End()
 32750Ôćĺ+	}
 32751Ôćĺ+}
 32752Ôćĺ+
 32753Ôćĺ+// RecordSessionMetrics records all telemetry metrics for a session.
 32754Ôćĺ+// This is a no-op when telemetry is disabled.
 32755Ôćĺ+func RecordSessionMetrics(ctx context.Context, stats SessionStats, duration time.Duration) {
 32756Ôćĺ+	if !metricsEnabled {
 32757Ôćĺ+		return
 32758Ôćĺ+	}
 32759Ôćĺ+	telemetryLogger().Debug("Recording session metrics",
 32760Ôćĺ+		"sessionId", stats.SessionID,
 32761Ôćĺ+		"agent", stats.AgentName,
 32762Ôćĺ+		"exchanges", stats.ExchangeCount,
 32763Ôćĺ+		"messages", stats.MessageCount,
 32764Ôćĺ+		"tools", stats.ToolCount,
 32765Ôćĺ+		"duration", duration,
 32766Ôćĺ+		"inputTokens", stats.TokenUsage.InputTokens,
 32767Ôćĺ+		"outputTokens", stats.TokenUsage.OutputTokens,
 32768Ôćĺ+	)
 32769Ôćĺ+	recordSessionProcessed(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID)
 32770Ôćĺ+	recordExchanges(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.ExchangeCount))
 32771Ôćĺ+	recordMessages(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.MessageCount))
 32772Ôćĺ+	recordToolUsage(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.ToolCount))
 32773Ôćĺ+	recordProcessingDuration(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, duration)
 32774Ôćĺ+	recordTokenUsage(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, stats.TokenUsage)
 32775Ôćĺ+}
 32776Ôćĺ+
 32777Ôćĺ+// --- Exchange Helpers ---
 32778Ôćĺ+
 32779Ôćĺ+// extractToolInfo extracts tool usage information from an exchange.
 32780Ôćĺ+// Returns: comma-separated unique tool names, comma-separated unique tool types, and total tool count.
 32781Ôćĺ+func extractToolInfo(exchange schema.Exchange) (toolNames string, toolTypes string, toolCount int) {
 32782Ôćĺ+	nameSet := make(map[string]bool)
 32783Ôćĺ+	typeSet := make(map[string]bool)
 32784Ôćĺ+
 32785Ôćĺ+	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
 32786Ôćĺ+		if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.Name != "" {
 32787Ôćĺ+			toolCount++
 32788Ôćĺ+			nameSet[msg.Tool.Name] = true
 32789Ôćĺ+			if msg.Tool.Type != "" {
 32790Ôćĺ+				typeSet[msg.Tool.Type] = true
 32791Ôćĺ+			}
 32792Ôćĺ+		}
 32793Ôćĺ+	}
 32794Ôćĺ+
 32795Ôćĺ+	// Build unique tool names list (sorted for deterministic output)
 32796Ôćĺ+	var names []string
 32797Ôćĺ+	for n := range nameSet {
 32798Ôćĺ+		names = append(names, n)
 32799Ôćĺ+	}
 32800Ôćĺ+	sort.Strings(names)
 32801Ôćĺ+
 32802Ôćĺ+	// Build unique tool types list (sorted for deterministic output)
 32803Ôćĺ+	var types []string
 32804Ôćĺ+	for t := range typeSet {
 32805Ôćĺ+		types = append(types, t)
 32806Ôćĺ+	}
 32807Ôćĺ+	sort.Strings(types)
 32808Ôćĺ+
 32809Ôćĺ+	return strings.Join(names, ","), strings.Join(types, ","), toolCount
 32810Ôćĺ+}
 32811Ôćĺ+
 32812Ôćĺ+// extractUserPromptText finds the first user message in an exchange and returns its text content.
 32813Ôćĺ+func extractUserPromptText(exchange schema.Exchange) string {
 32814Ôćĺ+	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
 32815Ôćĺ+		if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {
 32816Ôćĺ+			// Concatenate all text content parts
 32817Ôćĺ+			var text string
 32818Ôćĺ+			for _, part := range msg.Content {
 32819Ôćĺ+				if part.Type == schema.ContentTypeText {
 32820Ôćĺ+					if text != "" {
 32821Ôćĺ+						text += "\n"
 32822Ôćĺ+					}
 32823Ôćĺ+					text += part.Text
 32824Ôćĺ+				}
 32825Ôćĺ+			}
 32826Ôćĺ+			return text
 32827Ôćĺ+		}
 32828Ôćĺ+	}
 32829Ôćĺ+	return ""
 32830Ôćĺ+}
 32831Ôćĺ+
 32832Ôćĺ+// extractModel finds the model used in an exchange by looking at agent messages.
 32833Ôćĺ+// Returns the model from the first agent message that has one set.
 32834Ôćĺ+func extractModel(exchange schema.Exchange) string {
 32835Ôćĺ+	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
 32836Ôćĺ+		if msg.Role == schema.RoleAgent && msg.Model != "" {
 32837Ôćĺ+			return msg.Model
 32838Ôćĺ+		}
 32839Ôćĺ+	}
 32840Ôćĺ+	return ""
 32841Ôćĺ+}
 32842Ôćĺ+
 32843Ôćĺ+// --- Counting Helpers ---
 32844Ôćĺ+
 32845Ôćĺ+// countSessionMessages counts total messages across all exchanges.
 32846Ôćĺ+func countSessionMessages(exchanges []schema.Exchange) int {
 32847Ôćĺ+	count := 0
 32848Ôćĺ+	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
 32849Ôćĺ+		count += len(exchange.Messages)
 32850Ôćĺ+	}
 32851Ôćĺ+	return count
 32852Ôćĺ+}
 32853Ôćĺ+
 32854Ôćĺ+// countSessionTools counts total tools and unique tool types across all exchanges.
 32855Ôćĺ+// Returns: total tool count, unique tool type count.
 32856Ôćĺ+func countSessionTools(exchanges []schema.Exchange) (toolCount int, toolTypeCount int) {
 32857Ôćĺ+	typeSet := make(map[string]bool)
 32858Ôćĺ+
 32859Ôćĺ+	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
 32860Ôćĺ+		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
 32861Ôćĺ+			if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.Name != "" {
 32862Ôćĺ+				toolCount++
 32863Ôćĺ+				if msg.Tool.Type != "" {
 32864Ôćĺ+					typeSet[msg.Tool.Type] = true
 32865Ôćĺ+				}
 32866Ôćĺ+			}
 32867Ôćĺ+		}
 32868Ôćĺ+	}
 32869Ôćĺ+
 32870Ôćĺ+	return toolCount, len(typeSet)
 32871Ôćĺ+}
 32872Ôćĺ+
 32873Ôćĺ+// countExchangeTokens aggregates token usage for a single exchange.
 32874Ôćĺ+// Handles Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Droid CLI specific token fields.
 32875Ôćĺ+func countExchangeTokens(exchange schema.Exchange) TokenUsage {
 32876Ôćĺ+	var usage TokenUsage
 32877Ôćĺ+	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
 32878Ôćĺ+		if msg.Usage != nil {
 32879Ôćĺ+			// Common fields (all providers)
 32880Ôćĺ+			usage.InputTokens += msg.Usage.InputTokens
 32881Ôćĺ+			usage.OutputTokens += msg.Usage.OutputTokens
 32882Ôćĺ+			// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
 32883Ôćĺ+			usage.CacheCreationInputTokens += msg.Usage.CacheCreationInputTokens
 32884Ôćĺ+			usage.CacheReadInputTokens += msg.Usage.CacheReadInputTokens
 32885Ôćĺ+			// Codex CLI specific
 32886Ôćĺ+			usage.CachedInputTokens += msg.Usage.CachedInputTokens
 32887Ôćĺ+			usage.ReasoningOutputTokens += msg.Usage.ReasoningOutputTokens
 32888Ôćĺ+			// Gemini CLI specific
 32889Ôćĺ+			usage.CachedTokens += msg.Usage.CachedTokens
 32890Ôćĺ+			usage.ThoughtTokens += msg.Usage.ThoughtTokens
 32891Ôćĺ+			usage.ToolTokens += msg.Usage.ToolTokens
 32892Ôćĺ+			// Droid CLI specific
 32893Ôćĺ+			usage.ThinkingTokens += msg.Usage.ThinkingTokens
 32894Ôćĺ+		}
 32895Ôćĺ+	}
 32896Ôćĺ+	return usage
 32897Ôćĺ+}
 32898Ôćĺ+
 32899Ôćĺ+// countSessionTokens aggregates token usage across all exchanges in a session.
 32900Ôćĺ+// Handles Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Droid CLI specific token fields.
 32901Ôćĺ+func countSessionTokens(exchanges []schema.Exchange) TokenUsage {
 32902Ôćĺ+	var total TokenUsage
 32903Ôćĺ+	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
 32904Ôćĺ+		exchangeUsage := countExchangeTokens(exchange)
 32905Ôćĺ+		// Common fields (all providers)
 32906Ôćĺ+		total.InputTokens += exchangeUsage.InputTokens
 32907Ôćĺ+		total.OutputTokens += exchangeUsage.OutputTokens
 32908Ôćĺ+		// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
 32909Ôćĺ+		total.CacheCreationInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CacheCreationInputTokens
 32910Ôćĺ+		total.CacheReadInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CacheReadInputTokens
 32911Ôćĺ+		// Codex CLI specific
 32912Ôćĺ+		total.CachedInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CachedInputTokens
 32913Ôćĺ+		total.ReasoningOutputTokens += exchangeUsage.ReasoningOutputTokens
 32914Ôćĺ+		// Gemini CLI specific
 32915Ôćĺ+		total.CachedTokens += exchangeUsage.CachedTokens
 32916Ôćĺ+		total.ThoughtTokens += exchangeUsage.ThoughtTokens
 32917Ôćĺ+		total.ToolTokens += exchangeUsage.ToolTokens
 32918Ôćĺ+		// Droid CLI specific
 32919Ôćĺ+		total.ThinkingTokens += exchangeUsage.ThinkingTokens
 32920Ôćĺ+	}
 32921Ôćĺ+	return total
```

 33171Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-3-opus"},
 33172Ôćĺ+				},
 33173Ôćĺ+			},
 33174Ôćĺ+			want: "claude-3-opus",
 33175Ôćĺ+		},
 33176Ôćĺ+		{
 33177Ôćĺ+			name: "first agent message with model is used",
 33178Ôćĺ+			exchange: schema.Exchange{
 33179Ôćĺ+				Messages: []schema.Message{
 33180Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleUser},
 33181Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "first-model"},
 33182Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "second-model"},
 33183Ôćĺ+				},
 33184Ôćĺ+			},
 33185Ôćĺ+			want: "first-model",
 33186Ôćĺ+		},
 33187Ôćĺ+		{
 33188Ôćĺ+			name: "agent message without model skipped",
 33189Ôćĺ+			exchange: schema.Exchange{
 33190Ôćĺ+				Messages: []schema.Message{
 33191Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: ""},
 33192Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "actual-model"},
 33193Ôćĺ+				},
 33194Ôćĺ+			},
 33195Ôćĺ+			want: "actual-model",
 33196Ôćĺ+		},
 33197Ôćĺ+	}
 33198Ôćĺ+
 33199Ôćĺ+	for _, tt := range tests {
 33200Ôćĺ+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
 33201Ôćĺ+			got := extractModel(tt.exchange)
 33202Ôćĺ+			if got != tt.want {
 33203Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("extractModel() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
 33204Ôćĺ+			}
 33205Ôćĺ+		})
 33206Ôćĺ+	}
 33207Ôćĺ+}

 33209Ôćĺ+// --- countSessionTools tests ---
 33210Ôćĺ+
 33211Ôćĺ+func TestCountSessionTools(t *testing.T) {
 33212Ôćĺ+	tests := []struct {
 33213Ôćĺ+		name              string
 33214Ôćĺ+		exchanges         []schema.Exchange
 33215Ôćĺ+		wantToolCount     int
 33216Ôćĺ+		wantToolTypeCount int
 33217Ôćĺ+	}{
 33218Ôćĺ+		{
 33219Ôćĺ+			name:              "empty exchanges",
 33220Ôćĺ+			exchanges:         []schema.Exchange{},
 33221Ôćĺ+			wantToolCount:     0,
 33222Ôćĺ+			wantToolTypeCount: 0,
 33223Ôćĺ+		},
 33224Ôćĺ+		{
 33225Ôćĺ+			name: "no tools",
 33226Ôćĺ+			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
 33227Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{{Role: schema.RoleUser}, {Role: schema.RoleAgent}}},
 33228Ôćĺ+			},
 33229Ôćĺ+			wantToolCount:     0,
 33230Ôćĺ+			wantToolTypeCount: 0,
 33231Ôćĺ+		},
 33232Ôćĺ+		{
 33233Ôćĺ+			name: "single tool",
 33234Ôćĺ+			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
 33235Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{
 33236Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
 33237Ôćĺ+				}},
 33238Ôćĺ+			},
 33239Ôćĺ+			wantToolCount:     1,
 33240Ôćĺ+			wantToolTypeCount: 1,
 33241Ôćĺ+		},
 33242Ôćĺ+		{
 33243Ôćĺ+			name: "tools across multiple exchanges",
 33244Ôćĺ+			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
 33245Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{
 33246Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
 33247Ôćĺ+				}},
 33248Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{
 33249Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: "write"}},
 33250Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Bash", Type: "shell"}},
 33251Ôćĺ+				}},
 33252Ôćĺ+			},
 33253Ôćĺ+			wantToolCount:     3,
 33254Ôćĺ+			wantToolTypeCount: 3,
 33255Ôćĺ+		},
 33256Ôćĺ+		{
 33257Ôćĺ+			name: "duplicate tool types counted once",
 33258Ôćĺ+			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
 33259Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{
 33260Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
 33261Ôćĺ+					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Glob", Type: "read"}},
 33262Ôćĺ+				}},
 33263Ôćĺ+			},
 33264Ôćĺ+			wantToolCount:     2,
 33265Ôćĺ+			wantToolTypeCount: 1, // both are "read" type
 33266Ôćĺ+		},
 33267Ôćĺ+	}
 33268Ôćĺ+
 33269Ôćĺ+	for _, tt := range tests {
 33270Ôćĺ+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
 33271Ôćĺ+			gotCount, gotTypeCount := countSessionTools(tt.exchanges)
 33272Ôćĺ+			if gotCount != tt.wantToolCount {
 33273Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("countSessionTools() toolCount = %d, want %d", gotCount, tt.wantToolCount)
 33274Ôćĺ+			}
 33275Ôćĺ+			if gotTypeCount != tt.wantToolTypeCount {
 33276Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("countSessionTools() toolTypeCount = %d, want %d", gotTypeCount, tt.wantToolTypeCount)
 33277Ôćĺ+			}
 33278Ôćĺ+		})
 33279Ôćĺ+	}
 33280Ôćĺ+}

 33454Ôćĺ+func TestCountSessionTokens(t *testing.T) {

 33482Ôćĺ+				}},

 33497Ôćĺ+					}},
 33498Ôćĺ+				}},
 33499Ôćĺ+				{Messages: []schema.Message{

 33501Ôćĺ+						OutputTokens:         50,

 33512Ôćĺ+				CachedTokens:             20,

 33528Ôćĺ+// --- computeExchangeStats tests ---
 33529Ôćĺ+
 33530Ôćĺ+func Test_computeExchangeStats(t *testing.T) {
 33531Ôćĺ+	tests := []struct {
 33532Ôćĺ+		name     string
 33533Ôćĺ+		exchange schema.Exchange
 33534Ôćĺ+		idx      int
 33535Ôćĺ+		want     ExchangeStats
 33536Ôćĺ+	}{
 33537Ôćĺ+		{
 33538Ôćĺ+			name:     "empty exchange",
 33539Ôćĺ+			exchange: schema.Exchange{ExchangeID: "ex-1"},
 33540Ôćĺ+			idx:      0,
 33541Ôćĺ+			want: ExchangeStats{
 33542Ôćĺ+				ExchangeID:  "ex-1",
 33543Ôćĺ+				ExchangeIdx: 0,
 33544Ôćĺ+			},
 33545Ôćĺ+		},
 33546Ôćĺ+		{
 33547Ôćĺ+			name: "full exchange",
 33548Ôćĺ+			exchange: schema.Exchange{
 33549Ôćĺ+				ExchangeID: "ex-123",
 33550Ôćĺ+				StartTime:  "2024-01-01T10:00:00Z",
 33551Ôćĺ+				EndTime:    "2024-01-01T10:05:00Z",
 33552Ôćĺ+				Messages: []schema.Message{
 33553Ôćĺ+					{
 33554Ôćĺ+						Role: schema.RoleUser,
 33555Ôćĺ+						Content: []schema.ContentPart{
 33556Ôćĺ+							{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "Write a function"},
 33557Ôćĺ+						},
 33558Ôćĺ+					},
 33559Ôćĺ+					{
 33560Ôćĺ+						Role:  schema.RoleAgent,
 33561Ôćĺ+						Model: "claude-3-sonnet",
 33562Ôćĺ+						Tool:  &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: "write"},
 33563Ôćĺ+						Usage: &schema.Usage{InputTokens: 100, OutputTokens: 200},
 33564Ôćĺ+					},
 33565Ôćĺ+					{
 33566Ôćĺ+						Role:  schema.RoleAgent,
 33567Ôćĺ+						Tool:  &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Bash", Type: "shell"},
 33568Ôćĺ+						Usage: &schema.Usage{InputTokens: 50, OutputTokens: 30},
 33569Ôćĺ+					},
 33570Ôćĺ+				},
 33571Ôćĺ+			},
 33572Ôćĺ+			idx: 2,
 33573Ôćĺ+			want: ExchangeStats{
 33574Ôćĺ+				ExchangeID:   "ex-123",
 33575Ôćĺ+				ExchangeIdx:  2,
 33576Ôćĺ+				PromptText:   "Write a function",
 33577Ôćĺ+				StartTime:    "2024-01-01T10:00:00Z",
 33578Ôćĺ+				EndTime:      "2024-01-01T10:05:00Z",
 33579Ôćĺ+				MessageCount: 3,
 33580Ôćĺ+				ToolNames:    "Bash,Write",
 33581Ôćĺ+				ToolTypes:    "shell,write",
 33582Ôćĺ+				ToolCount:    2,
 33583Ôćĺ+				TokenUsage:   TokenUsage{InputTokens: 150, OutputTokens: 230},
 33584Ôćĺ+				Model:        "claude-3-sonnet",
 33585Ôćĺ+			},
 33586Ôćĺ+		},
 33587Ôćĺ+	}
 33588Ôćĺ+
 33589Ôćĺ+	for _, tt := range tests {
 33590Ôćĺ+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
 33591Ôćĺ+			got := computeExchangeStats(tt.exchange, tt.idx)
 33592Ôćĺ+			if got.ExchangeID != tt.want.ExchangeID {
 33593Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("ExchangeID = %q, want %q", got.ExchangeID, tt.want.ExchangeID)
 33594Ôćĺ+			}
 33595Ôćĺ+			if got.ExchangeIdx != tt.want.ExchangeIdx {
 33596Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("ExchangeIdx = %d, want %d", got.ExchangeIdx, tt.want.ExchangeIdx)
 33597Ôćĺ+			}
 33598Ôćĺ+			if got.PromptText != tt.want.PromptText {
 33599Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("PromptText = %q, want %q", got.PromptText, tt.want.PromptText)
 33600Ôćĺ+			}
 33601Ôćĺ+			if got.StartTime != tt.want.StartTime {
 33602Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("StartTime = %q, want %q", got.StartTime, tt.want.StartTime)
 33603Ôćĺ+			}
 33604Ôćĺ+			if got.EndTime != tt.want.EndTime {
 33605Ôćĺ+				t.Errorf("EndTime = %q, want %q", got.EndTime, tt.want.EndTime)
 33606Ôćĺ+			}
 33607Ôćĺ+			if got.MessageCount != tt.want.MessageCount {

 33609Ôćĺ+			}
 33610Ôćĺ+			if got.ToolNames != tt.want.ToolNames {

 33612Ôćĺ+			}

 33615Ôćĺ+			}

 33770Ôćĺ+// Package telemetry provides OpenTelemetry trace and metric initialization for the SpecStory CLI.
 33771Ôćĺ+// It follows the same idempotent-init pattern as pkg/analytics: first call to Init wins,
 33772Ôćĺ+// disabled path uses the OTel no-op provider (zero overhead, no nil checks needed).
 33773Ôćĺ+package telemetry
 33774Ôćĺ+
 33775Ôćĺ+import (
 33776Ôćĺ+	"context"
 33777Ôćĺ+	"crypto/sha256"
 33778Ôćĺ+	"fmt"
 33779Ôćĺ+	"log/slog"
 33780Ôćĺ+	"net/url"
 33781Ôćĺ+	"os"
 33782Ôćĺ+	"strings"
 33783Ôćĺ+	"sync"
 33784Ôćĺ+	"time"
 33785Ôćĺ+

 33787Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute"
 33788Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/otlp/otlpmetric/otlpmetricgrpc"
 33789Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/otlp/otlptrace/otlptracegrpc"
 33790Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/metric"
 33791Ôćĺ+	sdkmetric "go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/metric"
 33792Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/resource"
 33793Ôćĺ+	sdktrace "go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/trace"
 33794Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace"
 33795Ôćĺ+	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace/noop"
 33796Ôćĺ+)
 33797Ôćĺ+
 33798Ôćĺ+func telemetryLogger() *slog.Logger {
 33799Ôćĺ+	return slog.Default().With("component", "telemetry")
 33800Ôćĺ+}
 33801Ôćĺ+
 33802Ôćĺ+const (
 33803Ôćĺ+	// defaultEndpoint is the OTLP gRPC collector address used when
 33804Ôćĺ+	// OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set but Options.Endpoint is empty.
 33805Ôćĺ+	defaultEndpoint = "localhost:4317"
 33806Ôćĺ+
 33807Ôćĺ+	// metricExportInterval is how often metrics are exported to the collector.
 33808Ôćĺ+	metricExportInterval = 10 * time.Second
 33809Ôćĺ+)
 33810Ôćĺ+
 33811Ôćĺ+// Options configures telemetry initialisation.
 33812Ôćĺ+type Options struct {
 33813Ôćĺ+	ServiceName string // OTel service.name resource attribute (default "specstory-cli")
 33814Ôćĺ+	Endpoint    string // OTLP gRPC collector address (default "localhost:4317")
 33815Ôćĺ+	Enabled     bool   // When false, Init is a no-op and the global no-op provider is used
 33816Ôćĺ+}
 33817Ôćĺ+
 33818Ôćĺ+var (
 33819Ôćĺ+	initOnce       sync.Once
 33820Ôćĺ+	traceProvider  *sdktrace.TracerProvider
 33821Ôćĺ+	meterProvider  *sdkmetric.MeterProvider
 33822Ôćĺ+	meter          metric.Meter
 33823Ôćĺ+	metricsEnabled bool
 33824Ôćĺ+
 33825Ôćĺ+	// Metric instruments
 33826Ôćĺ+	sessionsProcessed  metric.Int64Counter
 33827Ôćĺ+	exchangesProcessed metric.Int64Counter
 33828Ôćĺ+	messagesProcessed  metric.Int64Counter
 33829Ôćĺ+	toolsUsed          metric.Int64Counter
 33830Ôćĺ+	processingDuration metric.Float64Histogram
 33831Ôćĺ+
 33832Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics (common)
 33833Ôćĺ+	inputTokensTotal  metric.Int64Counter
 33834Ôćĺ+	outputTokensTotal metric.Int64Counter
 33835Ôćĺ+
 33836Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics (Claude Code specific)
 33837Ôćĺ+	cacheCreationTokens metric.Int64Counter
 33838Ôćĺ+	cacheReadTokens     metric.Int64Counter
 33839Ôćĺ+
 33840Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics (Codex CLI specific)
 33841Ôćĺ+	cachedInputTokens     metric.Int64Counter
 33842Ôćĺ+	reasoningOutputTokens metric.Int64Counter
 33843Ôćĺ+
 33844Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics (Gemini CLI specific)
 33845Ôćĺ+	cachedTokens  metric.Int64Counter
 33846Ôćĺ+	thoughtTokens metric.Int64Counter
 33847Ôćĺ+	toolTokens    metric.Int64Counter
 33848Ôćĺ+
 33849Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics (Droid CLI specific)
 33850Ôćĺ+	thinkingTokens metric.Int64Counter
 33851Ôćĺ+
 33852Ôćĺ+	// Common attributes parsed from OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES to include on all metrics
 33853Ôćĺ+	commonMetricAttrs []attribute.KeyValue
 33854Ôćĺ+)
 33855Ôćĺ+
 33856Ôćĺ+// parseResourceAttributes parses OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var into attribute.KeyValue slice.
 33857Ôćĺ+// Format: "key1=value1,key2=value2"
 33858Ôćĺ+func parseResourceAttributes() []attribute.KeyValue {
 33859Ôćĺ+	envVal := os.Getenv("OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES")
 33860Ôćĺ+	if envVal == "" {
 33861Ôćĺ+		return nil
 33862Ôćĺ+	}
 33863Ôćĺ+
 33864Ôćĺ+	var attrs []attribute.KeyValue
 33865Ôćĺ+	pairs := strings.Split(envVal, ",")
 33866Ôćĺ+	for _, pair := range pairs {
 33867Ôćĺ+		pair = strings.TrimSpace(pair)
 33868Ôćĺ+		if pair == "" {
 33869Ôćĺ+			continue
 33870Ôćĺ+		}
 33871Ôćĺ+		kv := strings.SplitN(pair, "=", 2)
 33872Ôćĺ+		if len(kv) == 2 {
 33873Ôćĺ+			key := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])
 33874Ôćĺ+			value := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])
 33875Ôćĺ+			if key != "" {
 33876Ôćĺ+				attrs = append(attrs, attribute.String(key, value))
 33877Ôćĺ+			}
 33878Ôćĺ+		}
 33879Ôćĺ+	}
 33880Ôćĺ+	return attrs
 33881Ôćĺ+}
 33882Ôćĺ+
 33883Ôćĺ+// parseEndpoint normalises an OTLP endpoint string into a bare host:port suitable
 33884Ôćĺ+// for otlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint and a flag indicating whether TLS should be disabled.
 33885Ôćĺ+//
 33886Ôćĺ+// Accepted formats:
 33887Ôćĺ+//
 33888Ôćĺ+//	"localhost:4317"              Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",  insecure=true
 33889Ôćĺ+//	"http://localhost:4317"       Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",  insecure=true
 33890Ôćĺ+//	"https://collector.example:4317" Ôćĺ host="collector.example:4317", insecure=false
 33891Ôćĺ+//	""                            Ôćĺ host=defaultEndpoint,  insecure=true
 33892Ôćĺ+func parseEndpoint(raw string) (host string, insecure bool) {
 33893Ôćĺ+	if raw == "" {
 33894Ôćĺ+		return defaultEndpoint, true
 33895Ôćĺ+	}
 33896Ôćĺ+
 33897Ôćĺ+	u, err := url.Parse(raw)
 33898Ôćĺ+	if err != nil || u.Host == "" {
 33899Ôćĺ+		// Not a URL ÔÇö treat the whole string as host:port (bare address).
 33900Ôćĺ+		return raw, true
 33901Ôćĺ+	}
 33902Ôćĺ+
 33903Ôćĺ+	// Parsed successfully as a URL with a scheme.
 33904Ôćĺ+	host = u.Host
 33905Ôćĺ+	insecure = u.Scheme != "https"
 33906Ôćĺ+	return host, insecure
 33907Ôćĺ+}
 33908Ôćĺ+
 33909Ôćĺ+// Init configures the OTel tracing and metrics subsystem. Thread-safe and idempotent ÔÇö only the
 33910Ôćĺ+// first call takes effect. When Enabled is false the global no-op provider remains
 33911Ôćĺ+// active, so callers can emit spans/events and record metrics safely with zero overhead.
 33912Ôćĺ+func Init(ctx context.Context, opts Options) error {
 33913Ôćĺ+	var initErr error
 33914Ôćĺ+	initOnce.Do(func() {
 33915Ôćĺ+		if !opts.Enabled {
 33916Ôćĺ+			telemetryLogger().Info("Telemetry disabled, using no-op provider")
 33917Ôćĺ+			return
 33918Ôćĺ+		}
 33919Ôćĺ+
 33920Ôćĺ+		// Route OTel SDK errors (export failures, connection issues) through slog
 33921Ôćĺ+		// so they appear in debug logs alongside our own telemetry messages.
 33922Ôćĺ+		otel.SetErrorHandler(otel.ErrorHandlerFunc(func(err error) {
 33923Ôćĺ+			telemetryLogger().Warn("OTel SDK error", "error", err)
 33924Ôćĺ+		}))
 33925Ôćĺ+
 33926Ôćĺ+		serviceName := opts.ServiceName
 33927Ôćĺ+		if serviceName == "" {
 33928Ôćĺ+			serviceName = "specstory-cli"
 33929Ôćĺ+		}
 33930Ôćĺ+
 33931Ôćĺ+		// Parse the endpoint to extract a bare host:port for WithEndpoint and
 33932Ôćĺ+		// determine TLS mode from the scheme. OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is
 33933Ôćĺ+		// conventionally a full URL (e.g. "http://localhost:4317"), but
 33934Ôćĺ+		// WithEndpoint expects just "host:port".
 33935Ôćĺ+		host, insecure := parseEndpoint(opts.Endpoint)
 33936Ôćĺ+
 33937Ôćĺ+		// Create shared resource for both traces and metrics.
 33938Ôćĺ+		// Use resource.New() with detectors to pick up OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var.
 33939Ôćĺ+		res, err := resource.New(ctx,
 33940Ôćĺ+			resource.WithFromEnv(),      // OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES and OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
 33941Ôćĺ+			resource.WithTelemetrySDK(), // telemetry.sdk.* attributes
 33942Ôćĺ+			resource.WithHost(),         // host.* attributes
 33943Ôćĺ+			resource.WithAttributes(attribute.String("service.name", serviceName)), // explicit service name (can override env)
 33944Ôćĺ+		)
 33945Ôćĺ+		if err != nil {
 33946Ôćĺ+			initErr = fmt.Errorf("create OTel resource: %w", err)
 33947Ôćĺ+			return
 33948Ôćĺ+		}
 33949Ôćĺ+
 33950Ôćĺ+		// Log the resource attributes for debugging
 33951Ôćĺ+		telemetryLogger().Debug("Resource attributes created",
 33952Ôćĺ+			"attributes", res.Attributes(),
 33953Ôćĺ+			"OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES", os.Getenv("OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES"),
 33954Ôćĺ+		)
 33955Ôćĺ+
 33956Ôćĺ+		// Initialize tracing
 33957Ôćĺ+		if err := initTracing(ctx, host, insecure, res); err != nil {
 33958Ôćĺ+			initErr = err
 33959Ôćĺ+			return
 33960Ôćĺ+		}
 33961Ôćĺ+
 33962Ôćĺ+		// Initialize metrics
 33963Ôćĺ+		if err := initMetrics(ctx, host, insecure, res); err != nil {
 33964Ôćĺ+			// Roll back tracing initialization to avoid half-initialized state
 33965Ôćĺ+			telemetryLogger().Warn("Metrics init failed, rolling back tracing", "error", err)
 33966Ôćĺ+			if traceProvider != nil {
 33967Ôćĺ+				if shutdownErr := traceProvider.Shutdown(ctx); shutdownErr != nil {
 33968Ôćĺ+					telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to shutdown trace provider during rollback", "error", shutdownErr)
 33969Ôćĺ+				}
 33970Ôćĺ+				traceProvider = nil
 33971Ôćĺ+				// Use no-op provider instead of nil per OTel documentation
 33972Ôćĺ+				otel.SetTracerProvider(noop.NewTracerProvider())
 33973Ôćĺ+			}
 33974Ôćĺ+			initErr = err
 33975Ôćĺ+			return
 33976Ôćĺ+		}
 33977Ôćĺ+
 33978Ôćĺ+		metricsEnabled = true
 33979Ôćĺ+
 33980Ôćĺ+		// Parse OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES and store as common metric attributes.
 33981Ôćĺ+		// This ensures resource attributes appear as metric tags in backends like Datadog
 33982Ôćĺ+		// that may not automatically promote resource-level attributes to metric tags.
 33983Ôćĺ+		commonMetricAttrs = parseResourceAttributes()
 33984Ôćĺ+		if len(commonMetricAttrs) > 0 {
 33985Ôćĺ+			telemetryLogger().Debug("Parsed OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES for metrics",
 33986Ôćĺ+				"count", len(commonMetricAttrs),
 33987Ôćĺ+				"attributes", commonMetricAttrs,
 33988Ôćĺ+			)
 33989Ôćĺ+		}
 33990Ôćĺ+
 33991Ôćĺ+		telemetryLogger().Info("Telemetry initialised",
 33992Ôćĺ+			"endpoint", host,
 33993Ôćĺ+			"insecure", insecure,
 33994Ôćĺ+			"serviceName", serviceName,
 33995Ôćĺ+		)
 33996Ôćĺ+	})
 33997Ôćĺ+
 33998Ôćĺ+	return initErr
 33999Ôćĺ+}
 34000Ôćĺ+
 34001Ôćĺ+// initTracing sets up the trace provider and exporter.
 34002Ôćĺ+func initTracing(ctx context.Context, host string, insecure bool, res *resource.Resource) error {
 34003Ôćĺ+	exporterOpts := []otlptracegrpc.Option{
 34004Ôćĺ+		otlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint(host),
 34005Ôćĺ+	}
 34006Ôćĺ+	if insecure {
 34007Ôćĺ+		exporterOpts = append(exporterOpts, otlptracegrpc.WithInsecure())
 34008Ôćĺ+	}
 34009Ôćĺ+
 34010Ôćĺ+	exporter, err := otlptracegrpc.New(ctx, exporterOpts...)
 34011Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34012Ôćĺ+		return fmt.Errorf("create OTLP trace exporter: %w", err)
 34013Ôćĺ+	}
 34014Ôćĺ+
 34015Ôćĺ+	traceProvider = sdktrace.NewTracerProvider(
 34016Ôćĺ+		sdktrace.WithBatcher(exporter),
 34017Ôćĺ+		sdktrace.WithResource(res),
 34018Ôćĺ+	)
 34019Ôćĺ+	otel.SetTracerProvider(traceProvider)
 34020Ôćĺ+
 34021Ôćĺ+	return nil
 34022Ôćĺ+}
 34023Ôćĺ+
 34024Ôćĺ+// initMetrics sets up the meter provider, exporter, and metric instruments.
 34025Ôćĺ+func initMetrics(ctx context.Context, host string, insecure bool, res *resource.Resource) error {
 34026Ôćĺ+	exporterOpts := []otlpmetricgrpc.Option{
 34027Ôćĺ+		otlpmetricgrpc.WithEndpoint(host),
 34028Ôćĺ+	}
 34029Ôćĺ+	if insecure {
 34030Ôćĺ+		exporterOpts = append(exporterOpts, otlpmetricgrpc.WithInsecure())
 34031Ôćĺ+	}
 34032Ôćĺ+
 34033Ôćĺ+	exporter, err := otlpmetricgrpc.New(ctx, exporterOpts...)
 34034Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34035Ôćĺ+		return fmt.Errorf("create OTLP metric exporter: %w", err)
 34036Ôćĺ+	}
 34037Ôćĺ+
 34038Ôćĺ+	meterProvider = sdkmetric.NewMeterProvider(
 34039Ôćĺ+		sdkmetric.WithResource(res),
 34040Ôćĺ+		sdkmetric.WithReader(sdkmetric.NewPeriodicReader(exporter,
 34041Ôćĺ+			sdkmetric.WithInterval(metricExportInterval),
 34042Ôćĺ+		)),
 34043Ôćĺ+	)
 34044Ôćĺ+	otel.SetMeterProvider(meterProvider)
 34045Ôćĺ+
 34046Ôćĺ+	meter = meterProvider.Meter("specstory-cli")
 34047Ôćĺ+
 34048Ôćĺ+	// Initialize metric instruments
 34049Ôćĺ+	if err := initMetricInstruments(); err != nil {
 34050Ôćĺ+		return fmt.Errorf("create metric instruments: %w", err)
 34051Ôćĺ+	}
 34052Ôćĺ+
 34053Ôćĺ+	return nil
 34054Ôćĺ+}
 34055Ôćĺ+
 34056Ôćĺ+// initMetricInstruments creates all the metric instruments used by the CLI.
 34057Ôćĺ+func initMetricInstruments() error {
 34058Ôćĺ+	var err error
 34059Ôćĺ+
 34060Ôćĺ+	sessionsProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.sessions.processed",
 34061Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Number of sessions processed"),
 34062Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{session}"),
 34063Ôćĺ+	)

 34065Ôćĺ+		return err
 34066Ôćĺ+	}
 34067Ôćĺ+
 34068Ôćĺ+	exchangesProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.exchanges.processed",
 34069Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Number of exchanges processed across all sessions"),
 34070Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{exchange}"),
 34071Ôćĺ+	)
 34072Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34073Ôćĺ+		return err
 34074Ôćĺ+	}
 34075Ôćĺ+
 34076Ôćĺ+	messagesProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.messages.processed",
 34077Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Number of messages processed across all sessions"),
 34078Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{message}"),
 34079Ôćĺ+	)
 34080Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34081Ôćĺ+		return err
 34082Ôćĺ+	}
 34083Ôćĺ+
 34084Ôćĺ+	toolsUsed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tools.used",
 34085Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Number of tool invocations across all sessions"),
 34086Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{invocation}"),
 34087Ôćĺ+	)
 34088Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34089Ôćĺ+		return err
 34090Ôćĺ+	}
 34091Ôćĺ+
 34092Ôćĺ+	processingDuration, err = meter.Float64Histogram("specstory.session.duration",
 34093Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Session processing duration"),
 34094Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("s"),
 34095Ôćĺ+	)
 34096Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34097Ôćĺ+		return err
 34098Ôćĺ+	}
 34099Ôćĺ+
 34100Ôćĺ+	// Token usage metrics
 34101Ôćĺ+	inputTokensTotal, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.input",
 34102Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total input tokens consumed across all sessions"),
 34103Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34104Ôćĺ+	)
 34105Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34106Ôćĺ+		return err
 34107Ôćĺ+	}
 34108Ôćĺ+
 34109Ôćĺ+	outputTokensTotal, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.output",
 34110Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total output tokens generated across all sessions"),
 34111Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34112Ôćĺ+	)
 34113Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34114Ôćĺ+		return err
 34115Ôćĺ+	}
 34116Ôćĺ+
 34117Ôćĺ+	cacheCreationTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.cache_creation",
 34118Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total tokens written to cache across all sessions"),
 34119Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34120Ôćĺ+	)
 34121Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34122Ôćĺ+		return err
 34123Ôćĺ+	}
 34124Ôćĺ+
 34125Ôćĺ+	cacheReadTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.cache_read",
 34126Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total tokens read from cache across all sessions (Claude)"),
 34127Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34128Ôćĺ+	)
 34129Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34130Ôćĺ+		return err
 34131Ôćĺ+	}
 34132Ôćĺ+
 34133Ôćĺ+	// Codex CLI specific counters
 34134Ôćĺ+	cachedInputTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.cached_input",
 34135Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total cached input tokens across all sessions (Codex)"),
 34136Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34137Ôćĺ+	)
 34138Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34139Ôćĺ+		return err
 34140Ôćĺ+	}
 34141Ôćĺ+
 34142Ôćĺ+	reasoningOutputTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.reasoning_output",
 34143Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total reasoning output tokens across all sessions (Codex)"),
 34144Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34145Ôćĺ+	)
 34146Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
 34147Ôćĺ+		return err
 34148Ôćĺ+	}
 34149Ôćĺ+
 34150Ôćĺ+	// Gemini CLI specific counters
 34151Ôćĺ+	cachedTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.cached",
 34152Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total cached input tokens across all sessions (Gemini)"),
 34153Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34154Ôćĺ+	)
 34155Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {

 34157Ôćĺ+	}
 34158Ôćĺ+
 34159Ôćĺ+	thoughtTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.thought",

 34163Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {

 34167Ôćĺ+	toolTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.tool",

 34170Ôćĺ+	)
 34171Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {

 34174Ôćĺ+
 34175Ôćĺ+	// Droid CLI specific counters
 34176Ôćĺ+	thinkingTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.thinking",
 34177Ôćĺ+		metric.WithDescription("Total thinking/reasoning tokens across all sessions (Droid)"),
 34178Ôćĺ+		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
 34179Ôćĺ+	)
 34180Ôćĺ+	if err != nil {
