    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ// parseResourceAttributes parses OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var into attribute.KeyValue slice.
    88Ôćĺ// Format: "key1=value1,key2=value2"
    89Ôćĺfunc parseResourceAttributes() []attribute.KeyValue {
    90Ôćĺ	envVal := os.Getenv("OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES")
    91Ôćĺ	if envVal == "" {
    92Ôćĺ		return nil
    93Ôćĺ	}
    94Ôćĺ
    95Ôćĺ	var attrs []attribute.KeyValue
    96Ôćĺ	pairs := strings.Split(envVal, ",")
    97Ôćĺ	for _, pair := range pairs {
    98Ôćĺ		pair = strings.TrimSpace(pair)
    99Ôćĺ		if pair == "" {
   100Ôćĺ			continue
   101Ôćĺ		}
   102Ôćĺ		kv := strings.SplitN(pair, "=", 2)
   103Ôćĺ		if len(kv) == 2 {
   104Ôćĺ			key := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])
   105Ôćĺ			value := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])
   106Ôćĺ			if key != "" {
   107Ôćĺ				attrs = append(attrs, attribute.String(key, value))
   108Ôćĺ			}
   109Ôćĺ		}
   110Ôćĺ	}
   111Ôćĺ	return attrs
   112Ôćĺ}
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ// parseEndpoint normalises an OTLP endpoint string into a bare host:port suitable
   115Ôćĺ// for otlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint and a flag indicating whether TLS should be disabled.
   116Ôćĺ//
   117Ôćĺ// Accepted formats:
   118Ôćĺ//
   119Ôćĺ//	"localhost:4317"              Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",  insecure=true
   120Ôćĺ//	"http://localhost:4317"       Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",  insecure=true
   121Ôćĺ//	"https://collector.example:4317" Ôćĺ host="collector.example:4317", insecure=false
   122Ôćĺ//	""                            Ôćĺ host=defaultEndpoint,  insecure=true
   123Ôćĺfunc parseEndpoint(raw string) (host string, insecure bool) {
   124Ôćĺ	if raw == "" {
   125Ôćĺ		return defaultEndpoint, true
   126Ôćĺ	}
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ	u, err := url.Parse(raw)
   129Ôćĺ	if err != nil || u.Host == "" {
   130Ôćĺ		// Not a URL ÔÇö treat the whole string as host:port (bare address).
   131Ôćĺ		return raw, true
   132Ôćĺ	}
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ	// Parsed successfully as a URL with a scheme.
   135Ôćĺ	host = u.Host
   136Ôćĺ	insecure = u.Scheme != "https"
   137Ôćĺ	return host, insecure
   138Ôćĺ}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ// Init configures the OTel tracing and metrics subsystem. Thread-safe and idempotent ÔÇö only the
   141Ôćĺ// first call takes effect. When Enabled is false the global no-op provider remains
   142Ôćĺ// active, so callers can emit spans/events and record metrics safely with zero overhead.
   143Ôćĺfunc Init(ctx context.Context, opts Options) error {
   144Ôćĺ	var initErr error
   145Ôćĺ	initOnce.Do(func() {
   146Ôćĺ		if !opts.Enabled {
   147Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Info("Telemetry disabled, using no-op provider")
   148Ôćĺ			return
   149Ôćĺ		}
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ		// Route OTel SDK errors (export failures, connection issues) through slog
   152Ôćĺ		// so they appear in debug logs alongside our own telemetry messages.
   153Ôćĺ		otel.SetErrorHandler(otel.ErrorHandlerFunc(func(err error) {
   154Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("OTel SDK error", "error", err)
   155Ôćĺ		}))
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ		serviceName := opts.ServiceName
   158Ôćĺ		if serviceName == "" {
   159Ôćĺ			serviceName = "specstory-cli"
   160Ôćĺ		}
   161Ôćĺ
   162Ôćĺ		// Parse the endpoint to extract a bare host:port for WithEndpoint and
   163Ôćĺ		// determine TLS mode from the scheme. OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is
   164Ôćĺ		// conventionally a full URL (e.g. "http://localhost:4317"), but
   165Ôćĺ		// WithEndpoint expects just "host:port".
   166Ôćĺ		host, insecure := parseEndpoint(opts.Endpoint)
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ		// Create shared resource for both traces and metrics.
   169Ôćĺ		// Use resource.New() with detectors to pick up OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var.
   170Ôćĺ		res, err := resource.New(ctx,
   171Ôćĺ			resource.WithFromEnv(),      // OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES and OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
   172Ôćĺ			resource.WithTelemetrySDK(), // telemetry.sdk.* attributes
   173Ôćĺ			resource.WithHost(),         // host.* attributes
   174Ôćĺ			resource.WithAttributes(attribute.String("service.name", serviceName)), // explicit service name (can override env)
   175Ôćĺ		)
   176Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
   177Ôćĺ			initErr = fmt.Errorf("create OTel resource: %w", err)
   178Ôćĺ			return
   179Ôćĺ		}
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ		// Log the resource attributes for debugging
   182Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Debug("Resource attributes created",
   183Ôćĺ			"attributes", res.Attributes(),
   184Ôćĺ			"OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES", os.Getenv("OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES"),
   185Ôćĺ		)
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ		// Initialize tracing
   188Ôćĺ		if err := initTracing(ctx, host, insecure, res); err != nil {
   189Ôćĺ			initErr = err
   190Ôćĺ			return
   191Ôćĺ		}
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ		// Initialize metrics
   194Ôćĺ		if err := initMetrics(ctx, host, insecure, res); err != nil {
   195Ôćĺ			// Roll back tracing initialization to avoid half-initialized state
   196Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Metrics init failed, rolling back tracing", "error", err)
   197Ôćĺ			if traceProvider != nil {
   198Ôćĺ				if shutdownErr := traceProvider.Shutdown(ctx); shutdownErr != nil {
   199Ôćĺ					telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to shutdown trace provider during rollback", "error", shutdownErr)
   200Ôćĺ				}
   201Ôćĺ				traceProvider = nil
   202Ôćĺ				// Use no-op provider instead of nil per OTel documentation
   203Ôćĺ				otel.SetTracerProvider(noop.NewTracerProvider())
   204Ôćĺ			}
   205Ôćĺ			initErr = err
   206Ôćĺ			return
   207Ôćĺ		}
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ		metricsEnabled = true
   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ		// Parse OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES and store as common metric attributes.
   212Ôćĺ		// This ensures resource attributes appear as metric tags in backends like Datadog
   213Ôćĺ		// that may not automatically promote resource-level attributes to metric tags.
   214Ôćĺ		commonMetricAttrs = parseResourceAttributes()
   215Ôćĺ		if len(commonMetricAttrs) > 0 {
   216Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Debug("Parsed OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES for metrics",
   217Ôćĺ				"count", len(commonMetricAttrs),
   218Ôćĺ				"attributes", commonMetricAttrs,
   219Ôćĺ			)
   220Ôćĺ		}
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Info("Telemetry initialised",
   223Ôćĺ			"endpoint", host,
   224Ôćĺ			"insecure", insecure,
   225Ôćĺ			"serviceName", serviceName,
   226Ôćĺ		)
   227Ôćĺ	})
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ	return initErr
   230Ôćĺ}
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ// initTracing sets up the trace provider and exporter.
   233Ôćĺfunc initTracing(ctx context.Context, host string, insecure bool, res *resource.Resource) error {
   234Ôćĺ	exporterOpts := []otlptracegrpc.Option{
   235Ôćĺ		otlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint(host),
   236Ôćĺ	}
   237Ôćĺ	if insecure {
   238Ôćĺ		exporterOpts = append(exporterOpts, otlptracegrpc.WithInsecure())
   239Ôćĺ	}
   240Ôćĺ
   241Ôćĺ	exporter, err := otlptracegrpc.New(ctx, exporterOpts...)
   242Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   243Ôćĺ		return fmt.Errorf("create OTLP trace exporter: %w", err)
   244Ôćĺ	}
   245Ôćĺ
   246Ôćĺ	traceProvider = sdktrace.NewTracerProvider(
   247Ôćĺ		sdktrace.WithBatcher(exporter),
   248Ôćĺ		sdktrace.WithResource(res),
   249Ôćĺ	)
   250Ôćĺ	otel.SetTracerProvider(traceProvider)
   251Ôćĺ
   252Ôćĺ	return nil
   253Ôćĺ}
   254Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ// initMetrics sets up the meter provider, exporter, and metric instruments.
   256Ôćĺfunc initMetrics(ctx context.Context, host string, insecure bool, res *resource.Resource) error {
   257Ôćĺ	exporterOpts := []otlpmetricgrpc.Option{
   258Ôćĺ		otlpmetricgrpc.WithEndpoint(host),
   259Ôćĺ	}
   260Ôćĺ	if insecure {
   261Ôćĺ		exporterOpts = append(exporterOpts, otlpmetricgrpc.WithInsecure())
   262Ôćĺ	}
   263Ôćĺ
   264Ôćĺ	exporter, err := otlpmetricgrpc.New(ctx, exporterOpts...)
   265Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   266Ôćĺ		return fmt.Errorf("create OTLP metric exporter: %w", err)
   267Ôćĺ	}
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ	meterProvider = sdkmetric.NewMeterProvider(
   270Ôćĺ		sdkmetric.WithResource(res),
   271Ôćĺ		sdkmetric.WithReader(sdkmetric.NewPeriodicReader(exporter,
   272Ôćĺ			sdkmetric.WithInterval(metricExportInterval),
   273Ôćĺ		)),
   274Ôćĺ	)
   275Ôćĺ	otel.SetMeterProvider(meterProvider)
   276Ôćĺ
   277Ôćĺ	meter = meterProvider.Meter("specstory-cli")
   278Ôćĺ
   279Ôćĺ	// Initialize metric instruments
   280Ôćĺ	if err := initMetricInstruments(); err != nil {
   281Ôćĺ		return fmt.Errorf("create metric instruments: %w", err)
   282Ôćĺ	}
   283Ôćĺ
   284Ôćĺ	return nil
   285Ôćĺ}
   286Ôćĺ
   287Ôćĺ// initMetricInstruments creates all the metric instruments used by the CLI.
   288Ôćĺfunc initMetricInstruments() error {
   289Ôćĺ	var err error
   290Ôćĺ
   291Ôćĺ	sessionsProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.sessions.processed",
   292Ôćĺ		metric.WithDescription("Number of sessions processed"),
   293Ôćĺ		metric.WithUnit("{session}"),
   294Ôćĺ	)
   295Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   296Ôćĺ		return err
   297Ôćĺ	}
   298Ôćĺ
   299Ôćĺ	exchangesProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.exchanges.processed",
   300Ôćĺ		metric.WithDescription("Number of exchanges processed across all sessions"),
   301Ôćĺ		metric.WithUnit("{exchange}"),
   302Ôćĺ	)
   303Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   304Ôćĺ		return err
   305Ôćĺ	}
   306Ôćĺ
   307Ôćĺ	messagesProcessed, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.messages.processed",
   308Ôćĺ		metric.WithDescription("Number of messages processed across all sessions"),
   309Ôćĺ		metric.WithUnit("{message}"),
   310Ôćĺ	)
   311Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   312Ôćĺ		return err
   313Ôćĺ	}

   417Ôćĺ
   418Ôćĺ// Shutdown flushes pending spans/metrics and shuts down both providers.
   419Ôćĺ// Safe to call even when Init was never called or telemetry is disabled.
   420Ôćĺ// For short-lived CLIs, this performs explicit ForceFlush calls before shutdown
   421Ôćĺ// to ensure all data is exported before the process exits.
   422Ôćĺfunc Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {
   423Ôćĺ	var errs []error
   424Ôćĺ
   425Ôćĺ	if traceProvider != nil {
   426Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Info("Flushing and shutting down trace provider")
   427Ôćĺ		// ForceFlush ensures all pending spans are exported before shutdown.
   428Ôćĺ		// This is critical for short-lived CLIs that may exit before the
   429Ôćĺ		// periodic exporter has time to run.
   430Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   431Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to flush trace provider", "error", err)
   432Ôćĺ		}
   433Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
   434Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("shutdown trace provider: %w", err))
   435Ôćĺ		}
   436Ôćĺ	}
   437Ôćĺ
   438Ôćĺ	if meterProvider != nil {
   439Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Info("Flushing and shutting down meter provider")
   440Ôćĺ		// ForceFlush ensures all pending metrics are exported before shutdown.
   441Ôćĺ		// The periodic reader has a 10-second interval, so without this,
   442Ôćĺ		// metrics from short CLI runs would never be sent.
   443Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   444Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to flush meter provider", "error", err)
   445Ôćĺ		}
   446Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
   447Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("shutdown meter provider: %w", err))
   448Ôćĺ		}
   449Ôćĺ	}
   450Ôćĺ
   451Ôćĺ	if len(errs) > 0 {
   452Ôćĺ		return errs[0] // Return first error
   453Ôćĺ	}
   454Ôćĺ	return nil
   455Ôćĺ}
   456Ôćĺ
   457Ôćĺ// ForceFlush explicitly flushes all pending spans and metrics.
   458Ôćĺ// Call this at the end of short-lived commands to ensure data is exported
   459Ôćĺ// before the command returns. Safe to call when telemetry is disabled.
   460Ôćĺfunc ForceFlush(ctx context.Context) error {
   461Ôćĺ	var errs []error
   462Ôćĺ
   463Ôćĺ	if traceProvider != nil {
   464Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Debug("Force flushing trace provider")
   465Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   466Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to force flush trace provider", "error", err)
   467Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, err)
   468Ôćĺ		}
   469Ôćĺ	}
   470Ôćĺ
   471Ôćĺ	if meterProvider != nil {
   472Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Debug("Force flushing meter provider")
   473Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   474Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to force flush meter provider", "error", err)
   475Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, err)
   476Ôćĺ		}
   477Ôćĺ	}
   478Ôćĺ
   479Ôćĺ	if len(errs) > 0 {
   480Ôćĺ		return errs[0]
   481Ôćĺ	}
   482Ôćĺ	return nil
   483Ôćĺ}
   484Ôćĺ
   485Ôćĺ// Tracer returns a named tracer from the global provider. When telemetry is
   486Ôćĺ// disabled the returned tracer is a no-op (standard OTel behaviour).
   487Ôćĺfunc Tracer(name string) trace.Tracer {
   488Ôćĺ	return otel.Tracer(name)
   489Ôćĺ}
   490Ôćĺ
   491Ôćĺ// Meter returns the meter instance. When telemetry is disabled, returns the
   492Ôćĺ// global no-op meter.
   493Ôćĺfunc Meter() metric.Meter {
   494Ôćĺ	if meter != nil {
   495Ôćĺ		return meter
   496Ôćĺ	}
   497Ôćĺ	return otel.Meter("specstory-cli")
   498Ôćĺ}
   499Ôćĺ
   500Ôćĺ// ContextWithSessionTrace returns a context that will cause any spans started
   501Ôćĺ// from it to share the same trace ID (derived deterministically from sessionID).
   502Ôćĺ// This groups all spans for a given session into a single trace, even across
   503Ôćĺ// multiple invocations (e.g., in autosave mode where processSingleSession is
   504Ôćĺ// called repeatedly as the session file grows).
   505Ôćĺfunc ContextWithSessionTrace(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) context.Context {
   506Ôćĺ	traceID := traceIDFromSessionID(sessionID)
   507Ôćĺ	spanID := spanIDFromSessionID(sessionID)
   508Ôćĺ
   509Ôćĺ	// Create a SpanContext with both deterministic trace ID and span ID.
   510Ôćĺ	// In OpenTelemetry-Go, a SpanContext is only valid if both TraceID and SpanID
   511Ôćĺ	// are valid. We mark it as Remote so child spans inherit the trace ID.
   512Ôćĺ	sc := trace.NewSpanContext(trace.SpanContextConfig{
   513Ôćĺ		TraceID:    traceID,
   514Ôćĺ		SpanID:     spanID,
   515Ôćĺ		TraceFlags: trace.FlagsSampled, // Ensure it's sampled
   516Ôćĺ		Remote:     true,               // Mark as remote parent
   517Ôćĺ	})
   518Ôćĺ
   519Ôćĺ	// Inject the SpanContext as a remote parent so child spans inherit the trace ID
   520Ôćĺ	return trace.ContextWithRemoteSpanContext(ctx, sc)
   521Ôćĺ}
   522Ôćĺ
   523Ôćĺ// traceIDFromSessionID generates a deterministic 16-byte trace ID from a session ID
   524Ôćĺ// by hashing the session ID with SHA-256 and taking the first 16 bytes.
   525Ôćĺfunc traceIDFromSessionID(sessionID string) trace.TraceID {
   526Ôćĺ	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(sessionID))
   527Ôćĺ	var traceID trace.TraceID
   528Ôćĺ	copy(traceID[:], hash[:16])
   529Ôćĺ	return traceID
   530Ôćĺ}
   531Ôćĺ
   532Ôćĺ// spanIDFromSessionID generates a deterministic 8-byte span ID from a session ID
   533Ôćĺ// by hashing the session ID with SHA-256 and taking bytes 16-24.
   534Ôćĺ// This ensures the span ID is different from the trace ID but still deterministic.
   535Ôćĺfunc spanIDFromSessionID(sessionID string) trace.SpanID {
   536Ôćĺ	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(sessionID))
   537Ôćĺ	var spanID trace.SpanID
   538Ôćĺ	copy(spanID[:], hash[16:24])
   539Ôćĺ	return spanID
   540Ôćĺ}
   541Ôćĺ
   542Ôćĺ// --- Metric Recording Functions ---
   543Ôćĺ// These are internal functions called by RecordSessionMetrics in session_helpers.go.
   544Ôćĺ// The metricsEnabled check is done once in RecordSessionMetrics.
   545Ôćĺ
   546Ôćĺ// buildMetricAttrs combines specific metric attributes with common attributes from OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES.
   547Ôćĺfunc buildMetricAttrs(specific ...attribute.KeyValue) []attribute.KeyValue {
   548Ôćĺ	attrs := make([]attribute.KeyValue, 0, len(specific)+len(commonMetricAttrs))
   549Ôćĺ	attrs = append(attrs, specific...)
   550Ôćĺ	attrs = append(attrs, commonMetricAttrs...)
   551Ôćĺ	return attrs
   552Ôćĺ}
   553Ôćĺ
   554Ôćĺ// recordSessionProcessed increments the sessions processed counter.
   555Ôćĺfunc recordSessionProcessed(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string) {
   556Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   557Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   558Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   559Ôćĺ	)
   560Ôćĺ	sessionsProcessed.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   561Ôćĺ}
   562Ôćĺ
   563Ôćĺ// recordExchanges increments the exchanges counter by the given count.
   564Ôćĺfunc recordExchanges(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   565Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   566Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   567Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   568Ôćĺ	)
   569Ôćĺ	exchangesProcessed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   570Ôćĺ}
   571Ôćĺ
   572Ôćĺ// recordMessages increments the messages counter by the given count.
   573Ôćĺfunc recordMessages(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   574Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   575Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   576Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   577Ôćĺ	)
   578Ôćĺ	messagesProcessed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   579Ôćĺ}
   580Ôćĺ
   581Ôćĺ// recordToolUsage increments the tool usage counter by the given count.
   582Ôćĺfunc recordToolUsage(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   583Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   584Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   585Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   586Ôćĺ	)
   587Ôćĺ	toolsUsed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   588Ôćĺ}
   589Ôćĺ
   590Ôćĺ// recordProcessingDuration records the session processing duration.
   591Ôćĺfunc recordProcessingDuration(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, duration time.Duration) {
   592Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   593Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   594Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   595Ôćĺ	)
   596Ôćĺ	processingDuration.Record(ctx, duration.Seconds(), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   597Ôćĺ}
   598Ôćĺ
   599Ôćĺ// TokenUsage holds aggregated token counts for telemetry recording.
   600Ôćĺ// Different providers track different token types:
   601Ôćĺ//   - Claude Code: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CacheCreationInputTokens, CacheReadInputTokens
   602Ôćĺ//   - Codex CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedInputTokens, ReasoningOutputTokens
   603Ôćĺ//   - Gemini CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedTokens, ThoughtTokens, ToolTokens
   604Ôćĺ//   - Droid CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CacheCreationInputTokens, CacheReadInputTokens, ThinkingTokens
   605Ôćĺtype TokenUsage struct {
   606Ôćĺ	// Common fields (all providers)
   607Ôćĺ	InputTokens  int
   608Ôćĺ	OutputTokens int
   609Ôćĺ
   610Ôćĺ	// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   611Ôćĺ	CacheCreationInputTokens int
   612Ôćĺ	CacheReadInputTokens     int
   613Ôćĺ
   614Ôćĺ	// Codex CLI specific
   615Ôćĺ	CachedInputTokens     int
   616Ôćĺ	ReasoningOutputTokens int
   617Ôćĺ
   618Ôćĺ	// Gemini CLI specific
   619Ôćĺ	CachedTokens  int
   620Ôćĺ	ThoughtTokens int
   621Ôćĺ	ToolTokens    int
   622Ôćĺ
   623Ôćĺ	// Droid CLI specific
   624Ôćĺ	ThinkingTokens int
   625Ôćĺ}
   626Ôćĺ
   627Ôćĺ// recordTokenUsage records token usage metrics for a session.
   628Ôćĺ// Only non-zero values are recorded.
   629Ôćĺfunc recordTokenUsage(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, usage TokenUsage) {
   630Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   631Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   632Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   633Ôćĺ	)
   634Ôćĺ
   635Ôćĺ	// Common metrics
   636Ôćĺ	if usage.InputTokens > 0 {

   638Ôćĺ	}
   639Ôćĺ	if usage.OutputTokens > 0 {
   640Ôćĺ		outputTokensTotal.Add(ctx, int64(usage.OutputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   641Ôćĺ	}
   642Ôćĺ
   643Ôćĺ	// Claude Code specific
   644Ôćĺ	if usage.CacheCreationInputTokens > 0 {
   645Ôćĺ		cacheCreationTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.CacheCreationInputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   646Ôćĺ	}
   647Ôćĺ	if usage.CacheReadInputTokens > 0 {
   648Ôćĺ		cacheReadTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.CacheReadInputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   649Ôćĺ	}
   650Ôćĺ
   651Ôćĺ	// Codex CLI specific
   652Ôćĺ	if usage.CachedInputTokens > 0 {
   653Ôćĺ		cachedInputTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.CachedInputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   654Ôćĺ	}
   655Ôćĺ	if usage.ReasoningOutputTokens > 0 {
   656Ôćĺ		reasoningOutputTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.ReasoningOutputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   657Ôćĺ	}
   658Ôćĺ
   659Ôćĺ	// Gemini CLI specific
   660Ôćĺ	if usage.CachedTokens > 0 {
   661Ôćĺ		cachedTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.CachedTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   662Ôćĺ	}
   663Ôćĺ	if usage.ThoughtTokens > 0 {
   664Ôćĺ		thoughtTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.ThoughtTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   665Ôćĺ	}
   666Ôćĺ	if usage.ToolTokens > 0 {

     1Ôćĺ// Package telemetry provides session statistics helpers for computing and recording
     2Ôćĺ// telemetry data from agent chat sessions.
     3Ôćĺpackage telemetry
     4Ôćĺ
     5Ôćĺimport (
     6Ôćĺ	"context"
     7Ôćĺ	"sort"
     8Ôćĺ	"strings"
     9Ôćĺ	"time"
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute"
    12Ôćĺ	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace"
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺ	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    15Ôćĺ	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
    16Ôćĺ)
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ// --- Session Statistics ---
    19Ôćĺ
    20Ôćĺ// SessionStats holds computed statistics for a session, used for telemetry and span attributes.
    21Ôćĺtype SessionStats struct {
    22Ôćĺ	AgentName     string
    23Ôćĺ	SessionID     string
    24Ôćĺ	ProjectPath   string
    25Ôćĺ	ExchangeCount int
    26Ôćĺ	MessageCount  int
    27Ôćĺ	ToolCount     int
    28Ôćĺ	ToolTypeCount int
    29Ôćĺ	TokenUsage    TokenUsage
    30Ôćĺ}
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ// ExchangeStats holds computed statistics for a single exchange, used for child span attributes.
    33Ôćĺtype ExchangeStats struct {
    34Ôćĺ	ExchangeID   string
    35Ôćĺ	ExchangeIdx  int
    36Ôćĺ	PromptText   string
    37Ôćĺ	StartTime    string
    38Ôćĺ	EndTime      string
    39Ôćĺ	MessageCount int
    40Ôćĺ	ToolNames    string
    41Ôćĺ	ToolTypes    string
    42Ôćĺ	ToolCount    int
    43Ôćĺ	TokenUsage   TokenUsage
    44Ôćĺ	Model        string // The model used for this exchange (from agent messages)
    45Ôćĺ}
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ// computeExchangeStats computes statistics for a single exchange.
    48Ôćĺfunc computeExchangeStats(exchange schema.Exchange, idx int) ExchangeStats {
    49Ôćĺ	toolNames, toolTypes, toolCount := extractToolInfo(exchange)
    50Ôćĺ	tokenUsage := countExchangeTokens(exchange)
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ	return ExchangeStats{
    53Ôćĺ		ExchangeID:   exchange.ExchangeID,
    54Ôćĺ		ExchangeIdx:  idx,
    55Ôćĺ		PromptText:   extractUserPromptText(exchange),
    56Ôćĺ		StartTime:    exchange.StartTime,
    57Ôćĺ		EndTime:      exchange.EndTime,
    58Ôćĺ		MessageCount: len(exchange.Messages),
    59Ôćĺ		ToolNames:    toolNames,
    60Ôćĺ		ToolTypes:    toolTypes,
    61Ôćĺ		ToolCount:    toolCount,
    62Ôćĺ		TokenUsage:   tokenUsage,
    63Ôćĺ		Model:        extractModel(exchange),
    64Ôćĺ	}
    65Ôćĺ}
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ// ComputeSessionStats computes all statistics for a session from its SessionData.
    68Ôćĺfunc ComputeSessionStats(agentName string, session *spi.AgentChatSession) SessionStats {
    69Ôćĺ	stats := SessionStats{
    70Ôćĺ		AgentName: agentName,
    71Ôćĺ		SessionID: session.SessionID,
    72Ôćĺ	}
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ	if session.SessionData != nil {
    75Ôćĺ		stats.ProjectPath = session.SessionData.WorkspaceRoot
    76Ôćĺ		stats.ExchangeCount = len(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
    77Ôćĺ		stats.MessageCount = countSessionMessages(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
    78Ôćĺ		stats.ToolCount, stats.ToolTypeCount = countSessionTools(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
    79Ôćĺ		stats.TokenUsage = countSessionTokens(session.SessionData.Exchanges)
    80Ôćĺ	}
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ	return stats
    83Ôćĺ}
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ// SetSessionSpanAttributes sets all standard session attributes on a span.
    86Ôćĺ// This sets session-level summary attributes only; exchange details are in child spans.
    87Ôćĺ// Token usage attributes are provider-specific and only non-zero values are meaningful.
    88Ôćĺfunc SetSessionSpanAttributes(span trace.Span, stats SessionStats) {
    89Ôćĺ	span.SetAttributes(
    90Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", stats.AgentName),
    91Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", stats.SessionID),
    92Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.exchange_count", stats.ExchangeCount),
    93Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.message_count", stats.MessageCount),
    94Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tool_count", stats.ToolCount),
    95Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tool_type_count", stats.ToolTypeCount),
    96Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.project.path", stats.ProjectPath),
    97Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - common (all providers)
    98Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.input", stats.TokenUsage.InputTokens),
    99Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.output", stats.TokenUsage.OutputTokens),
   100Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   101Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.cache_creation", stats.TokenUsage.CacheCreationInputTokens),
   102Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.cache_read", stats.TokenUsage.CacheReadInputTokens),
   103Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Codex CLI specific
   104Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.cached_input", stats.TokenUsage.CachedInputTokens),
   105Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.reasoning_output", stats.TokenUsage.ReasoningOutputTokens),
   106Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Gemini CLI specific
   107Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.cached", stats.TokenUsage.CachedTokens),
   108Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.thought", stats.TokenUsage.ThoughtTokens),
   109Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.tool", stats.TokenUsage.ToolTokens),
   110Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Droid CLI specific
   111Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.session.tokens.thinking", stats.TokenUsage.ThinkingTokens),
   112Ôćĺ	)
   113Ôćĺ}
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ// startExchangeSpan creates a child span for processing a single exchange.
   116Ôćĺ// The returned span should be ended by the caller when exchange processing is complete.
   117Ôćĺfunc startExchangeSpan(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, exchangeID string, idx int) (context.Context, trace.Span) {
   118Ôćĺ	return Tracer("specstory").Start(ctx, "process_exchange",
   119Ôćĺ		trace.WithAttributes(
   120Ôćĺ			attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   121Ôćĺ			attribute.String("specstory.exchange.id", exchangeID),
   122Ôćĺ			attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.index", idx),
   123Ôćĺ		),
   124Ôćĺ	)
   125Ôćĺ}
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺ// setExchangeSpanAttributes sets all attributes on an exchange span.
   128Ôćĺ// Token usage attributes are provider-specific and only non-zero values are meaningful.
   129Ôćĺfunc setExchangeSpanAttributes(span trace.Span, stats ExchangeStats) {
   130Ôćĺ	span.SetAttributes(
   131Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.id", stats.ExchangeID),
   132Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.index", stats.ExchangeIdx),
   133Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.model", stats.Model),
   134Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.prompt_text", stats.PromptText),
   135Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.start_time", stats.StartTime),
   136Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.end_time", stats.EndTime),
   137Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.message_count", stats.MessageCount),
   138Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.tools_used", stats.ToolNames),
   139Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.exchange.tool_types", stats.ToolTypes),
   140Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tool_count", stats.ToolCount),
   141Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - common (all providers)
   142Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.input", stats.TokenUsage.InputTokens),
   143Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.output", stats.TokenUsage.OutputTokens),
   144Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   145Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.cache_creation", stats.TokenUsage.CacheCreationInputTokens),
   146Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.cache_read", stats.TokenUsage.CacheReadInputTokens),
   147Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Codex CLI specific
   148Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.cached_input", stats.TokenUsage.CachedInputTokens),
   149Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.reasoning_output", stats.TokenUsage.ReasoningOutputTokens),
   150Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Gemini CLI specific
   151Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.cached", stats.TokenUsage.CachedTokens),
   152Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.thought", stats.TokenUsage.ThoughtTokens),
   153Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.tool", stats.TokenUsage.ToolTokens),
   154Ôćĺ		// Token usage attributes - Droid CLI specific
   155Ôćĺ		attribute.Int("specstory.exchange.tokens.thinking", stats.TokenUsage.ThinkingTokens),
   156Ôćĺ	)
   157Ôćĺ}
   158Ôćĺ
   159Ôćĺ// ProcessExchangeSpans creates child spans for all exchanges in a session.
   160Ôćĺ// This is a helper that creates spans, sets attributes, and ends them immediately
   161Ôćĺ// since exchange processing is retrospective (data already exists).
   162Ôćĺ// When noPrompts is true, the prompt_text attribute will be empty for privacy.
   163Ôćĺfunc ProcessExchangeSpans(ctx context.Context, stats SessionStats, exchanges []schema.Exchange, noPrompts bool) {
   164Ôćĺ	telemetryLogger().Debug("Creating exchange spans",
   165Ôćĺ		"sessionId", stats.SessionID,
   166Ôćĺ		"exchangeCount", len(exchanges),
   167Ôćĺ		"noPrompts", noPrompts,
   168Ôćĺ	)
   169Ôćĺ	for i, exchange := range exchanges {
   170Ôćĺ		_, exchangeSpan := startExchangeSpan(ctx, stats.SessionID, exchange.ExchangeID, i)
   171Ôćĺ		exchangeStats := computeExchangeStats(exchange, i)
   172Ôćĺ		if noPrompts {
   173Ôćĺ			exchangeStats.PromptText = ""
   174Ôćĺ		}
   175Ôćĺ		setExchangeSpanAttributes(exchangeSpan, exchangeStats)
   176Ôćĺ		exchangeSpan.End()
   177Ôćĺ	}
   178Ôćĺ}
   179Ôćĺ
   180Ôćĺ// RecordSessionMetrics records all telemetry metrics for a session.
   181Ôćĺ// This is a no-op when telemetry is disabled.
   182Ôćĺfunc RecordSessionMetrics(ctx context.Context, stats SessionStats, duration time.Duration) {
   183Ôćĺ	if !metricsEnabled {
   184Ôćĺ		return
   185Ôćĺ	}
   186Ôćĺ	telemetryLogger().Debug("Recording session metrics",
   187Ôćĺ		"sessionId", stats.SessionID,
   188Ôćĺ		"agent", stats.AgentName,
   189Ôćĺ		"exchanges", stats.ExchangeCount,
   190Ôćĺ		"messages", stats.MessageCount,
   191Ôćĺ		"tools", stats.ToolCount,
   192Ôćĺ		"duration", duration,
   193Ôćĺ		"inputTokens", stats.TokenUsage.InputTokens,
   194Ôćĺ		"outputTokens", stats.TokenUsage.OutputTokens,
   195Ôćĺ	)
   196Ôćĺ	recordSessionProcessed(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID)
   197Ôćĺ	recordExchanges(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.ExchangeCount))
   198Ôćĺ	recordMessages(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.MessageCount))
   199Ôćĺ	recordToolUsage(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, int64(stats.ToolCount))
   200Ôćĺ	recordProcessingDuration(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, duration)
   201Ôćĺ	recordTokenUsage(ctx, stats.AgentName, stats.SessionID, stats.TokenUsage)
   202Ôćĺ}
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ// --- Exchange Helpers ---
   205Ôćĺ
   206Ôćĺ// extractToolInfo extracts tool usage information from an exchange.
   207Ôćĺ// Returns: comma-separated unique tool names, comma-separated unique tool types, and total tool count.
   208Ôćĺfunc extractToolInfo(exchange schema.Exchange) (toolNames string, toolTypes string, toolCount int) {
   209Ôćĺ	nameSet := make(map[string]bool)
   210Ôćĺ	typeSet := make(map[string]bool)
   211Ôćĺ
   212Ôćĺ	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   213Ôćĺ		if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.Name != "" {
   214Ôćĺ			toolCount++
   215Ôćĺ			nameSet[msg.Tool.Name] = true
   216Ôćĺ			if msg.Tool.Type != "" {
   217Ôćĺ				typeSet[msg.Tool.Type] = true
   218Ôćĺ			}
   219Ôćĺ		}
   220Ôćĺ	}
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ	// Build unique tool names list (sorted for deterministic output)
   223Ôćĺ	var names []string
   224Ôćĺ	for n := range nameSet {
   225Ôćĺ		names = append(names, n)
   226Ôćĺ	}
   227Ôćĺ	sort.Strings(names)
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ	// Build unique tool types list (sorted for deterministic output)
   230Ôćĺ	var types []string
   231Ôćĺ	for t := range typeSet {
   232Ôćĺ		types = append(types, t)
   233Ôćĺ	}
   234Ôćĺ	sort.Strings(types)
   235Ôćĺ
   236Ôćĺ	return strings.Join(names, ","), strings.Join(types, ","), toolCount
   237Ôćĺ}
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ// extractUserPromptText finds the first user message in an exchange and returns its text content.
   240Ôćĺfunc extractUserPromptText(exchange schema.Exchange) string {
   241Ôćĺ	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   242Ôćĺ		if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {
   243Ôćĺ			// Concatenate all text content parts
   244Ôćĺ			var text string
   245Ôćĺ			for _, part := range msg.Content {
   246Ôćĺ				if part.Type == schema.ContentTypeText {
   247Ôćĺ					if text != "" {
   248Ôćĺ						text += "\n"
   249Ôćĺ					}
   250Ôćĺ					text += part.Text
   251Ôćĺ				}
   252Ôćĺ			}
   253Ôćĺ			return text
   254Ôćĺ		}
   255Ôćĺ	}
   256Ôćĺ	return ""
   257Ôćĺ}
   258Ôćĺ
   259Ôćĺ// extractModel finds the model used in an exchange by looking at agent messages.
   260Ôćĺ// Returns the model from the first agent message that has one set.
   261Ôćĺfunc extractModel(exchange schema.Exchange) string {
   262Ôćĺ	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   263Ôćĺ		if msg.Role == schema.RoleAgent && msg.Model != "" {
   264Ôćĺ			return msg.Model
   265Ôćĺ		}
   266Ôćĺ	}
   267Ôćĺ	return ""
   268Ôćĺ}
   269Ôćĺ
   270Ôćĺ// --- Counting Helpers ---
   271Ôćĺ
   272Ôćĺ// countSessionMessages counts total messages across all exchanges.
   273Ôćĺfunc countSessionMessages(exchanges []schema.Exchange) int {
   274Ôćĺ	count := 0
   275Ôćĺ	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
   276Ôćĺ		count += len(exchange.Messages)
   277Ôćĺ	}
   278Ôćĺ	return count
   279Ôćĺ}
   280Ôćĺ
   281Ôćĺ// countSessionTools counts total tools and unique tool types across all exchanges.
   282Ôćĺ// Returns: total tool count, unique tool type count.
   283Ôćĺfunc countSessionTools(exchanges []schema.Exchange) (toolCount int, toolTypeCount int) {
   284Ôćĺ	typeSet := make(map[string]bool)
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
   287Ôćĺ		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   288Ôćĺ			if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.Name != "" {
   289Ôćĺ				toolCount++
   290Ôćĺ				if msg.Tool.Type != "" {
   291Ôćĺ					typeSet[msg.Tool.Type] = true
   292Ôćĺ				}
   293Ôćĺ			}
   294Ôćĺ		}
   295Ôćĺ	}
   296Ôćĺ
   297Ôćĺ	return toolCount, len(typeSet)
   298Ôćĺ}
   299Ôćĺ
   300Ôćĺ// countExchangeTokens aggregates token usage for a single exchange.
   301Ôćĺ// Handles Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Droid CLI specific token fields.
   302Ôćĺfunc countExchangeTokens(exchange schema.Exchange) TokenUsage {
   303Ôćĺ	var usage TokenUsage
   304Ôćĺ	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   305Ôćĺ		if msg.Usage != nil {
   306Ôćĺ			// Common fields (all providers)
   307Ôćĺ			usage.InputTokens += msg.Usage.InputTokens
   308Ôćĺ			usage.OutputTokens += msg.Usage.OutputTokens
   309Ôćĺ			// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   310Ôćĺ			usage.CacheCreationInputTokens += msg.Usage.CacheCreationInputTokens
   311Ôćĺ			usage.CacheReadInputTokens += msg.Usage.CacheReadInputTokens
   312Ôćĺ			// Codex CLI specific
   313Ôćĺ			usage.CachedInputTokens += msg.Usage.CachedInputTokens
   314Ôćĺ			usage.ReasoningOutputTokens += msg.Usage.ReasoningOutputTokens
   315Ôćĺ			// Gemini CLI specific
   316Ôćĺ			usage.CachedTokens += msg.Usage.CachedTokens
   317Ôćĺ			usage.ThoughtTokens += msg.Usage.ThoughtTokens
   318Ôćĺ			usage.ToolTokens += msg.Usage.ToolTokens
   319Ôćĺ			// Droid CLI specific
   320Ôćĺ			usage.ThinkingTokens += msg.Usage.ThinkingTokens
   321Ôćĺ		}
   322Ôćĺ	}
   323Ôćĺ	return usage
   324Ôćĺ}
   325Ôćĺ
   326Ôćĺ// countSessionTokens aggregates token usage across all exchanges in a session.
   327Ôćĺ// Handles Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Droid CLI specific token fields.
   328Ôćĺfunc countSessionTokens(exchanges []schema.Exchange) TokenUsage {
   329Ôćĺ	var total TokenUsage
   330Ôćĺ	for _, exchange := range exchanges {
   331Ôćĺ		exchangeUsage := countExchangeTokens(exchange)
   332Ôćĺ		// Common fields (all providers)
   333Ôćĺ		total.InputTokens += exchangeUsage.InputTokens
   334Ôćĺ		total.OutputTokens += exchangeUsage.OutputTokens
   335Ôćĺ		// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   336Ôćĺ		total.CacheCreationInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CacheCreationInputTokens
   337Ôćĺ		total.CacheReadInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CacheReadInputTokens
   338Ôćĺ		// Codex CLI specific
   339Ôćĺ		total.CachedInputTokens += exchangeUsage.CachedInputTokens
   340Ôćĺ		total.ReasoningOutputTokens += exchangeUsage.ReasoningOutputTokens
   341Ôćĺ		// Gemini CLI specific
   342Ôćĺ		total.CachedTokens += exchangeUsage.CachedTokens
   343Ôćĺ		total.ThoughtTokens += exchangeUsage.ThoughtTokens
   344Ôćĺ		total.ToolTokens += exchangeUsage.ToolTokens
   345Ôćĺ		// Droid CLI specific
   346Ôćĺ		total.ThinkingTokens += exchangeUsage.ThinkingTokens
   347Ôćĺ	}
   348Ôćĺ	return total
   349Ôćĺ}

   131Ôćĺfunc TestExtractUserPromptText(t *testing.T) {

   134Ôćĺ		exchange schema.Exchange

   191Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Content: []schema.ContentPart{{Type: schema.ContentTypeText, Text: "second user message ignored"}}},

   206Ôćĺ
   207Ôćĺ	for _, tt := range tests {

   217Ôćĺ// --- extractModel tests ---
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺfunc TestExtractModel(t *testing.T) {
   220Ôćĺ	tests := []struct {
   221Ôćĺ		name     string
   222Ôćĺ		exchange schema.Exchange
   223Ôćĺ		want     string
   224Ôćĺ	}{
   225Ôćĺ		{
   226Ôćĺ			name:     "empty exchange",
   227Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{},
   228Ôćĺ			want:     "",
   229Ôćĺ		},
   230Ôćĺ		{
   231Ôćĺ			name: "no agent message",
   232Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{
   233Ôćĺ				Messages: []schema.Message{
   234Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleUser, Model: "should-be-ignored"},
   235Ôćĺ				},
   236Ôćĺ			},
   237Ôćĺ			want: "",
   238Ôćĺ		},
   239Ôćĺ		{
   240Ôćĺ			name: "agent message with model",
   241Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{
   242Ôćĺ				Messages: []schema.Message{
   243Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "claude-3-opus"},
   244Ôćĺ				},
   245Ôćĺ			},
   246Ôćĺ			want: "claude-3-opus",
   247Ôćĺ		},
   248Ôćĺ		{
   249Ôćĺ			name: "first agent message with model is used",
   250Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{
   251Ôćĺ				Messages: []schema.Message{
   252Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleUser},
   253Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "first-model"},
   254Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "second-model"},
   255Ôćĺ				},
   256Ôćĺ			},
   257Ôćĺ			want: "first-model",
   258Ôćĺ		},
   259Ôćĺ		{
   260Ôćĺ			name: "agent message without model skipped",
   261Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{
   262Ôćĺ				Messages: []schema.Message{
   263Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: ""},
   264Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Model: "actual-model"},
   265Ôćĺ				},
   266Ôćĺ			},
   267Ôćĺ			want: "actual-model",
   268Ôćĺ		},
   269Ôćĺ	}
   270Ôćĺ
   271Ôćĺ	for _, tt := range tests {
   272Ôćĺ		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   273Ôćĺ			got := extractModel(tt.exchange)
   274Ôćĺ			if got != tt.want {
   275Ôćĺ				t.Errorf("extractModel() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
   276Ôćĺ			}
   277Ôćĺ		})
   278Ôćĺ	}
   279Ôćĺ}

   281Ôćĺ// --- countSessionTools tests ---
   282Ôćĺ
   283Ôćĺfunc TestCountSessionTools(t *testing.T) {
   284Ôćĺ	tests := []struct {
   285Ôćĺ		name              string
   286Ôćĺ		exchanges         []schema.Exchange
   287Ôćĺ		wantToolCount     int
   288Ôćĺ		wantToolTypeCount int
   289Ôćĺ	}{
   290Ôćĺ		{
   291Ôćĺ			name:              "empty exchanges",
   292Ôćĺ			exchanges:         []schema.Exchange{},
   293Ôćĺ			wantToolCount:     0,
   294Ôćĺ			wantToolTypeCount: 0,
   295Ôćĺ		},
   296Ôćĺ		{
   297Ôćĺ			name: "no tools",
   298Ôćĺ			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
   299Ôćĺ				{Messages: []schema.Message{{Role: schema.RoleUser}, {Role: schema.RoleAgent}}},
   300Ôćĺ			},
   301Ôćĺ			wantToolCount:     0,
   302Ôćĺ			wantToolTypeCount: 0,
   303Ôćĺ		},
   304Ôćĺ		{
   305Ôćĺ			name: "single tool",
   306Ôćĺ			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
   307Ôćĺ				{Messages: []schema.Message{
   308Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
   309Ôćĺ				}},
   310Ôćĺ			},
   311Ôćĺ			wantToolCount:     1,
   312Ôćĺ			wantToolTypeCount: 1,
   313Ôćĺ		},
   314Ôćĺ		{
   315Ôćĺ			name: "tools across multiple exchanges",
   316Ôćĺ			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
   317Ôćĺ				{Messages: []schema.Message{
   318Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
   319Ôćĺ				}},
   320Ôćĺ				{Messages: []schema.Message{
   321Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Write", Type: "write"}},
   322Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Bash", Type: "shell"}},
   323Ôćĺ				}},
   324Ôćĺ			},
   325Ôćĺ			wantToolCount:     3,
   326Ôćĺ			wantToolTypeCount: 3,
   327Ôćĺ		},
   328Ôćĺ		{
   329Ôćĺ			name: "duplicate tool types counted once",
   330Ôćĺ			exchanges: []schema.Exchange{
   331Ôćĺ				{Messages: []schema.Message{
   332Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Read", Type: "read"}},
   333Ôćĺ					{Role: schema.RoleAgent, Tool: &schema.ToolInfo{Name: "Glob", Type: "read"}},
   334Ôćĺ				}},
   335Ôćĺ			},
   336Ôćĺ			wantToolCount:     2,
   337Ôćĺ			wantToolTypeCount: 1, // both are "read" type
   338Ôćĺ		},
   339Ôćĺ	}
   340Ôćĺ
   341Ôćĺ	for _, tt := range tests {
   342Ôćĺ		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   343Ôćĺ			gotCount, gotTypeCount := countSessionTools(tt.exchanges)
   344Ôćĺ			if gotCount != tt.wantToolCount {
   345Ôćĺ				t.Errorf("countSessionTools() toolCount = %d, want %d", gotCount, tt.wantToolCount)
   346Ôćĺ			}
   347Ôćĺ			if gotTypeCount != tt.wantToolTypeCount {
   348Ôćĺ				t.Errorf("countSessionTools() toolTypeCount = %d, want %d", gotTypeCount, tt.wantToolTypeCount)
   349Ôćĺ			}
   350Ôćĺ		})
   351Ôćĺ	}
   352Ôćĺ}
   353Ôćĺ

   526Ôćĺfunc TestCountSessionTokens(t *testing.T) {

   600Ôćĺ// --- computeExchangeStats tests ---
   601Ôćĺ
   602Ôćĺfunc Test_computeExchangeStats(t *testing.T) {
   603Ôćĺ	tests := []struct {
   604Ôćĺ		name     string
   605Ôćĺ		exchange schema.Exchange
   606Ôćĺ		idx      int
   607Ôćĺ		want     ExchangeStats
   608Ôćĺ	}{
   609Ôćĺ		{
   610Ôćĺ			name:     "empty exchange",
   611Ôćĺ			exchange: schema.Exchange{ExchangeID: "ex-1"},
   612Ôćĺ			idx:      0,
   613Ôćĺ			want: ExchangeStats{

     1Ôćĺ// Package config provides configuration management for the SpecStory CLI.
     2Ôćĺ// Configuration is loaded with the following priority (highest to lowest):
     3Ôćĺ//  1. CLI flags
     4Ôćĺ//  2. Local project config: .specstory/cli/config.toml
     5Ôćĺ//  3. User-level config: ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
     6Ôćĺ//
     7Ôćĺ// Note: For telemetry settings, environment variables (OTEL_*) take highest priority
     8Ôćĺ// per OpenTelemetry conventions.
     9Ôćĺpackage config
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺimport (
    12Ôćĺ	"fmt"
    13Ôćĺ	"log/slog"
    14Ôćĺ	"os"

    18Ôćĺ	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"

    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺconst (
    22Ôćĺ	// ConfigFileName is the name of the configuration file
    23Ôćĺ	ConfigFileName = "config.toml"
    24Ôćĺ	// SpecStoryDir is the directory name for SpecStory files
    25Ôćĺ	SpecStoryDir = ".specstory"
    26Ôćĺ	// CLIDir is the subdirectory for CLI-specific files (config, auth, etc.)
    27Ôćĺ	CLIDir = "cli"
    28Ôćĺ)
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ// defaultConfigTemplate is the content written to a newly created config file.
    31Ôćĺ// All options are commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
    32Ôćĺconst defaultConfigTemplate = `# SpecStory CLI Configuration
    33Ôćĺ#
    34Ôćĺ# {u This is the user-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
    35Ôćĺ# All settings here apply to every project unless overridden
    36Ôćĺ# by a project-level config at ./.specstory/cli/config.toml
    37Ôćĺ# or overridden by CLI flags.}
    38Ôćĺ# {p This is the project-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
    39Ôćĺ# All settings here apply to this project unless overridden by CLI flags.}
    40Ôćĺ#
    41Ôćĺ# Uncomment (remove the #) the line and edit any setting below to change the default behavior.
    42Ôćĺ# For more information, see: https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ[local_sync]
    45Ôćĺ# Write markdown files locally. (default: true)
    46Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --only-cloud-sync
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ# Custom output directory for markdown files.
    49Ôćĺ# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)
    50Ôćĺ# output_dir = "~/.specstory/history" # equivalent to --output-dir "~/.specstory/history"
    51Ôćĺ
    52Ôćĺ# Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (default: false, UTC)
    53Ôćĺ# local_time_zone = true # equivalent to --local-time-zone
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ[cloud_sync]
    56Ôćĺ# Sync session data to SpecStory Cloud. (default: true, when logged in to SpecStory Cloud)
    57Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-cloud-sync
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ[logging]
    60Ôćĺ# Custom output directory for debug data.
    61Ôćĺ# Default: ./.specstory/debug (relative to the project directory)
    62Ôćĺ# debug_dir = "~/.specstory/debug" # equivalent to --debug-dir "~/.specstory/debug"
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ# Error/warn/info output to stdout (default: false)
    65Ôćĺ# console = true # equivalent to --console
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ# Write logs to .specstory/debug/debug.log (default: false)
    68Ôćĺ# log = true # equivalent to --log        
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ# Debug-level output, requires console or log (default: false)
    71Ôćĺ# debug = true # equivalent to --debug 
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ# Suppress all non-error output (default: false)
    74Ôćĺ# silent = true	# equivalent to --silent
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ[version_check]
    77Ôćĺ# Check for new versions of the CLI on startup.
    78Ôćĺ# Default: true
    79Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-version-check
    80Ôćĺ
    81Ôćĺ[analytics]
    82Ôćĺ# Send anonymous product usage analytics to help improve SpecStory.
    83Ôćĺ# Default: true
    84Ôćĺ# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-usage-analytics
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺ[telemetry]
    87Ôćĺ# OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
    88Ôćĺ# endpoint = "localhost:4317"
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ# Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
    91Ôćĺ# service_name = "my-service-name"
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ# Include user prompt text in telemetry spans (default: true)
    94Ôćĺ# prompts = false
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ[providers]
    97Ôćĺ# Agent execution commands by provider (used by specstory run)
    98Ôćĺ# Pass custom flags (e.g. claude_cmd = "claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions")
    99Ôćĺ# Use of these is equivalent to -c "custom command"
   100Ôćĺ

   103Ôćĺ

   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ// Config represents the complete CLI configuration
   118Ôćĺtype Config struct {
   119Ôćĺ	VersionCheck VersionCheckConfig `toml:"version_check"`
   120Ôćĺ	CloudSync    CloudSyncConfig    `toml:"cloud_sync"`
   121Ôćĺ	LocalSync    LocalSyncConfig    `toml:"local_sync"`
   122Ôćĺ	Logging      LoggingConfig      `toml:"logging"`
   123Ôćĺ	Analytics    AnalyticsConfig    `toml:"analytics"`
   124Ôćĺ	Telemetry    TelemetryConfig    `toml:"telemetry"`
   125Ôćĺ	Providers    ProvidersConfig    `toml:"providers"`
   126Ôćĺ}
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ// VersionCheckConfig holds version check settings
   129Ôćĺtype VersionCheckConfig struct {
   130Ôćĺ	// Enabled controls whether to check for newer versions on startup
   131Ôćĺ	Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
   132Ôćĺ}
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ// CloudSyncConfig holds cloud sync settings
   135Ôćĺtype CloudSyncConfig struct {
   136Ôćĺ	// Enabled controls whether cloud sync is active
   137Ôćĺ	Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
   138Ôćĺ}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ// LocalSyncConfig holds local file sync settings
   141Ôćĺtype LocalSyncConfig struct {
   142Ôćĺ	// Enabled controls whether local markdown files are written
   143Ôćĺ	// When false, only cloud sync is performed (equivalent to --only-cloud-sync)
   144Ôćĺ	Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
   145Ôćĺ	// OutputDir is the custom output directory for markdown files
   146Ôćĺ	OutputDir string `toml:"output_dir"`
   147Ôćĺ	// LocalTimeZone enables local timezone for file name and content timestamps
   148Ôćĺ	LocalTimeZone *bool `toml:"local_time_zone"`
   149Ôćĺ}
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ// LoggingConfig holds logging settings
   152Ôćĺtype LoggingConfig struct {
   153Ôćĺ	// DebugDir is the custom output directory for debug data
   154Ôćĺ	DebugDir string `toml:"debug_dir"`
   155Ôćĺ	// Console enables error/warn/info output to stdout
   156Ôćĺ	Console *bool `toml:"console"`
   157Ôćĺ	// Log enables writing error/warn/info output to .specstory/debug/debug.log
   158Ôćĺ	Log *bool `toml:"log"`
   159Ôćĺ	// Debug enables debug-level output (requires Console or Log)
   160Ôćĺ	Debug *bool `toml:"debug"`
   161Ôćĺ	// Silent suppresses all non-error output
   162Ôćĺ	Silent *bool `toml:"silent"`
   163Ôćĺ}
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ// AnalyticsConfig holds analytics settings
   166Ôćĺtype AnalyticsConfig struct {
   167Ôćĺ	// Enabled controls whether usage analytics are sent
   168Ôćĺ	Enabled *bool `toml:"enabled"`
   169Ôćĺ}
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ// TelemetryConfig holds OpenTelemetry configuration.
   172Ôćĺ// Telemetry is enabled when Endpoint is non-empty (no separate "enabled" flag).
   173Ôćĺtype TelemetryConfig struct {
   174Ôćĺ	// Endpoint is the OTLP gRPC collector address (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
   175Ôćĺ	// Env var: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
   176Ôćĺ	Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint"`
   177Ôćĺ
   178Ôćĺ	// ServiceName overrides the default service name ("specstory-cli")
   179Ôćĺ	// Env var: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
   180Ôćĺ	ServiceName string `toml:"service_name"`
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ	// Prompts controls whether user prompt text is included in telemetry spans.
   183Ôćĺ	// Defaults to true (prompts are included). Set to false to exclude prompt text
   184Ôćĺ	// from the specstory.exchange.prompt_text attribute for privacy.
   185Ôćĺ	Prompts *bool `toml:"prompts"`
   186Ôćĺ}
   187Ôćĺ
   188Ôćĺ// ProvidersConfig holds custom agent execution commands by provider.
   189Ôćĺ// These are used by `specstory run` as the equivalent of the -c flag,
   190Ôćĺ// scoped to a specific provider.
   191Ôćĺtype ProvidersConfig struct {
   192Ôćĺ	ClaudeCmd string `toml:"claude_cmd"`
   193Ôćĺ	CodexCmd  string `toml:"codex_cmd"`
   194Ôćĺ	CursorCmd string `toml:"cursor_cmd"`
   195Ôćĺ	DroidCmd  string `toml:"droid_cmd"`
   196Ôćĺ	GeminiCmd string `toml:"gemini_cmd"`
   197Ôćĺ}
   198Ôćĺ
   199Ôćĺ// CLIOverrides holds CLI flag values that override config file settings.
   200Ôćĺ// These are applied after config files are loaded.
   201Ôćĺtype CLIOverrides struct {
   202Ôćĺ	// General
   203Ôćĺ	OutputDir     string
   204Ôćĺ	LocalTimeZone bool
   205Ôćĺ
   206Ôćĺ	// Version check
   207Ôćĺ	NoVersionCheck bool
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ	// Cloud sync
   210Ôćĺ	NoCloudSync   bool
   211Ôćĺ	OnlyCloudSync bool
   212Ôćĺ
   213Ôćĺ	// Logging
   214Ôćĺ	DebugDir string
   215Ôćĺ	Console  bool
   216Ôćĺ	Log      bool
   217Ôćĺ	Debug    bool
   218Ôćĺ	Silent   bool
   219Ôćĺ
   220Ôćĺ	// Analytics
   221Ôćĺ	NoAnalytics bool
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ	// Telemetry
   224Ôćĺ	TelemetryEndpoint    string
   225Ôćĺ	TelemetryServiceName string
   226Ôćĺ	NoTelemetryPrompts   bool
   227Ôćĺ}
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ// Load reads configuration from files and CLI flags.
   230Ôćĺ// Priority: CLI flags > local project config > user-level config
   231Ôćĺ// Note: For telemetry, OTEL_* env vars take highest priority per OTel conventions.
   232Ôćĺ//
   233Ôćĺ// Returns an error if a config file exists but cannot be parsed (TOML syntax error)
   234Ôćĺ// or cannot be read (permission denied, I/O error). Missing config files are not
   235Ôćĺ// treated as errors - they are simply skipped.
   236Ôćĺfunc Load(cliOverrides *CLIOverrides) (*Config, error) {
   237Ôćĺ	cfg := &Config{}
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ	// Load user-level config first (lowest priority)
   240Ôćĺ	userConfigPath := getUserConfigPath()
   241Ôćĺ	if userConfigPath != "" {
   242Ôćĺ		if err := loadTOMLFile(userConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
   243Ôćĺ			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
   244Ôćĺ				slog.Debug("No user-level config file found", "path", userConfigPath)
   245Ôćĺ				ensureDefaultUserConfig(userConfigPath)
   246Ôćĺ			} else {
   247Ôćĺ				// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
   248Ôćĺ				return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load user config %s: %w", userConfigPath, err)
   249Ôćĺ			}
   250Ôćĺ		} else {
   251Ôćĺ			slog.Debug("Loaded user-level config", "path", userConfigPath)
   252Ôćĺ		}
   253Ôćĺ	}
   254Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ	// Load local project config (overwrites user-level)
   256Ôćĺ	localConfigPath := getLocalConfigPath()
   257Ôćĺ	if localConfigPath != "" {
   258Ôćĺ		if err := loadTOMLFile(localConfigPath, cfg); err != nil {
   259Ôćĺ			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
   260Ôćĺ				slog.Debug("No local project config file found", "path", localConfigPath)
   261Ôćĺ			} else {
   262Ôćĺ				// Parse error or permission denied - return error to caller
   263Ôćĺ				return cfg, fmt.Errorf("failed to load project config %s: %w", localConfigPath, err)
   264Ôćĺ			}
   265Ôćĺ		} else {
   266Ôćĺ			slog.Debug("Loaded local project config", "path", localConfigPath)
   267Ôćĺ		}
   268Ôćĺ	}
   269Ôćĺ
   270Ôćĺ	// Apply CLI flag overrides (highest priority for most settings)
   271Ôćĺ	if cliOverrides != nil {
   272Ôćĺ		applyCLIOverrides(cfg, cliOverrides)
   273Ôćĺ	}
   274Ôćĺ
   275Ôćĺ	// Apply OTel environment variables (highest priority for telemetry only)
   276Ôćĺ	applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg)
   277Ôćĺ
   278Ôćĺ	return cfg, nil
   279Ôćĺ}
   280Ôćĺ
   281Ôćĺ// GetUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
   282Ôćĺfunc GetUserConfigPath() string {
   283Ôćĺ	return getUserConfigPath()
   284Ôćĺ}
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ// GetLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
   287Ôćĺfunc GetLocalConfigPath() string {
   288Ôćĺ	return getLocalConfigPath()
   289Ôćĺ}
   290Ôćĺ
   291Ôćĺ// getUserConfigPath returns the path to ~/.specstory/cli/config.toml
   292Ôćĺfunc getUserConfigPath() string {
   293Ôćĺ	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   294Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   295Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not determine home directory", "error", err)
   296Ôćĺ		return ""
   297Ôćĺ	}
   298Ôćĺ	return filepath.Join(home, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
   299Ôćĺ}
   300Ôćĺ
   301Ôćĺ// getLocalConfigPath returns the path to .specstory/cli/config.toml in the current directory
   302Ôćĺfunc getLocalConfigPath() string {
   303Ôćĺ	cwd, err := os.Getwd()
   304Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   305Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not determine current directory", "error", err)
   306Ôćĺ		return ""
   307Ôćĺ	}
   308Ôćĺ	return filepath.Join(cwd, SpecStoryDir, CLIDir, ConfigFileName)
   309Ôćĺ}
   310Ôćĺ
   311Ôćĺ// processTemplate processes the default config template for a given level
   312Ôćĺ// ("user" or "project"). Template markers {u ...} and {p ...} delimit content
   313Ôćĺ// specific to user-level or project-level config files respectively.
   314Ôćĺ//
   315Ôćĺ// For level "user": content inside {u ...} is kept (markers stripped),
   316Ôćĺ// content inside {p ...} is removed entirely.
   317Ôćĺ// For level "project": the opposite applies.
   318Ôćĺ//
   319Ôćĺ// Markers can span multiple lines. The opening marker ({u or {p) appears at
   320Ôćĺ// the start of a line (after optional whitespace/comment prefix), and the
   321Ôćĺ// closing } appears at the end of a line.
   322Ôćĺfunc processTemplate(template, level string) string {
   323Ôćĺ	var result strings.Builder
   324Ôćĺ	keepTag := "{u"
   325Ôćĺ	stripTag := "{p"
   326Ôćĺ	if level == "project" {
   327Ôćĺ		keepTag = "{p"
   328Ôćĺ		stripTag = "{u"
   329Ôćĺ	}
   330Ôćĺ
   331Ôćĺ	insideKeep := false
   332Ôćĺ	insideStrip := false
   333Ôćĺ
   334Ôćĺ	for line := range strings.SplitSeq(template, "\n") {
   335Ôćĺ		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
   336Ôćĺ		// Remove any leading "# " to inspect the marker
   337Ôćĺ		bare := strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "# ")
   338Ôćĺ
   339Ôćĺ		switch {
   340Ôćĺ		case insideStrip:
   341Ôćĺ			// Check for closing brace ÔÇö ends the stripped block
   342Ôćĺ			if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
   343Ôćĺ				insideStrip = false
   344Ôćĺ			}
   345Ôćĺ			// Drop the entire line regardless
   346Ôćĺ			continue
   347Ôćĺ
   348Ôćĺ		case insideKeep:
   349Ôćĺ			// Check for closing brace ÔÇö ends the kept block
   350Ôćĺ			if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
   351Ôćĺ				insideKeep = false
   352Ôćĺ				// Strip the trailing } from the line
   353Ôćĺ				idx := strings.LastIndex(line, "}")
   354Ôćĺ				line = line[:idx]
   355Ôćĺ				// Only emit if something remains after stripping
   356Ôćĺ				if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(line) == "#" {
   357Ôćĺ					continue
   358Ôćĺ				}
   359Ôćĺ			}
   360Ôćĺ			result.WriteString(line)
   361Ôćĺ			result.WriteString("\n")
   362Ôćĺ
   363Ôćĺ		case strings.HasPrefix(bare, keepTag+" "):
   364Ôćĺ			insideKeep = true
   365Ôćĺ			// prefix is everything before the bare marker (e.g. "# ")
   366Ôćĺ			prefix := line[:strings.Index(line, bare)]
   367Ôćĺ			// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
   368Ôćĺ			if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
   369Ôćĺ				insideKeep = false
   370Ôćĺ				// Extract content between tag and closing brace
   371Ôćĺ				content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1 : len(bare)-1])
   372Ôćĺ				reconstructed := prefix + content
   373Ôćĺ				if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
   374Ôćĺ					continue
   375Ôćĺ				}
   376Ôćĺ				result.WriteString(reconstructed)
   377Ôćĺ				result.WriteString("\n")
   378Ôćĺ			} else {
   379Ôćĺ				// Multi-line: strip the marker prefix, keep content after it
   380Ôćĺ				content := strings.TrimSpace(bare[len(keepTag)+1:])
   381Ôćĺ				reconstructed := prefix + content
   382Ôćĺ				if strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "" || strings.TrimSpace(reconstructed) == "#" {
   383Ôćĺ					continue
   384Ôćĺ				}
   385Ôćĺ				result.WriteString(reconstructed)
   386Ôćĺ				result.WriteString("\n")
   387Ôćĺ			}
   388Ôćĺ
   389Ôćĺ		case strings.HasPrefix(bare, stripTag+" "):
   390Ôćĺ			insideStrip = true
   391Ôćĺ			// Check if single-line block (closing } on same line)
   392Ôćĺ			if strings.HasSuffix(bare, "}") {
   393Ôćĺ				insideStrip = false
   394Ôćĺ			}
   395Ôćĺ			continue
   396Ôćĺ
   397Ôćĺ		default:
   398Ôćĺ			result.WriteString(line)
   399Ôćĺ			result.WriteString("\n")
   400Ôćĺ		}
   401Ôćĺ	}
   402Ôćĺ
   403Ôćĺ	// Remove trailing extra newline that accumulates from the split
   404Ôćĺ	out := result.String()
   405Ôćĺ	if strings.HasSuffix(out, "\n\n") && !strings.HasSuffix(template, "\n\n") {
   406Ôćĺ		out = out[:len(out)-1]
   407Ôćĺ	}
   408Ôćĺ	return out
   409Ôćĺ}
   410Ôćĺ
   411Ôćĺ// ensureDefaultUserConfig creates a default user-level config file with all
   412Ôćĺ// options commented out. This makes the config discoverable without changing
   413Ôćĺ// any behavior. Failures are silently ignored ÔÇö this is a convenience, not
   414Ôćĺ// a requirement.
   415Ôćĺfunc ensureDefaultUserConfig(path string) {
   416Ôćĺ	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
   417Ôćĺ	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
   418Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
   419Ôćĺ		return
   420Ôćĺ	}
   421Ôćĺ	processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "user")
   422Ôćĺ	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
   423Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
   424Ôćĺ		return
   425Ôćĺ	}
   426Ôćĺ	slog.Debug("Created default user config file", "path", path)
   427Ôćĺ}
   428Ôćĺ
   429Ôćĺ// EnsureDefaultProjectConfig creates a default project-level config file at
   430Ôćĺ// .specstory/cli/config.toml if one doesn't already exist. All options are
   431Ôćĺ// commented out so the file is self-documenting but inert.
   432Ôćĺ//
   433Ôćĺ// This should only be called from commands that imply active project work
   434Ôćĺ// (run, sync, watch) to avoid scattering config files in arbitrary directories.
   435Ôćĺ// Failures are silently ignored ÔÇö this is a convenience, not a requirement.
   436Ôćĺfunc EnsureDefaultProjectConfig() {
   437Ôćĺ	path := getLocalConfigPath()
   438Ôćĺ	if path == "" {
   439Ôćĺ		return
   440Ôćĺ	}
   441Ôćĺ	// Don't overwrite an existing file
   442Ôćĺ	if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
   443Ôćĺ		return
   444Ôćĺ	}
   445Ôćĺ	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
   446Ôćĺ	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
   447Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not create config directory", "path", dir, "error", err)
   448Ôćĺ		return
   449Ôćĺ	}
   450Ôćĺ	processed := processTemplate(defaultConfigTemplate, "project")
   451Ôćĺ	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(processed), 0644); err != nil {
   452Ôćĺ		slog.Debug("Could not write default config file", "path", path, "error", err)
   453Ôćĺ		return
   454Ôćĺ	}
   455Ôćĺ	slog.Debug("Created default project config file", "path", path)
   456Ôćĺ}
   457Ôćĺ
   458Ôćĺ// loadTOMLFile reads a TOML file and decodes it into the config struct.
   459Ôćĺ// Fields are merged (later calls overwrite earlier values).
   460Ôćĺfunc loadTOMLFile(path string, cfg *Config) error {
   461Ôćĺ	_, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, cfg)
   462Ôćĺ	return err
   463Ôćĺ}
   464Ôćĺ
   465Ôćĺ// applyTelemetryEnvOverrides applies OTel environment variable overrides.
   466Ôćĺ// Per OTel conventions, these take highest priority for telemetry settings.
   467Ôćĺfunc applyTelemetryEnvOverrides(cfg *Config) {
   468Ôćĺ	if endpoint := os.Getenv("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT"); endpoint != "" {
   469Ôćĺ		cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = endpoint
   470Ôćĺ	}
   471Ôćĺ
   472Ôćĺ	if serviceName := os.Getenv("OTEL_SERVICE_NAME"); serviceName != "" {
   473Ôćĺ		cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = serviceName
   474Ôćĺ	}
   475Ôćĺ
   476Ôćĺ	// Note: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED is handled in IsTelemetryEnabled() rather than
   477Ôćĺ	// stored in the config struct, since there is no Enabled field ÔÇö telemetry
   478Ôćĺ	// is enabled when an endpoint is configured.
   479Ôćĺ}
   480Ôćĺ
   481Ôćĺ// ConfigValidationResult holds the result of validating a config file.
   482Ôćĺtype ConfigValidationResult struct {
   483Ôćĺ	Path        string   // Path to the config file
   484Ôćĺ	Exists      bool     // Whether the file exists
   485Ôćĺ	ValidTOML   bool     // Whether the file is valid TOML
   486Ôćĺ	ParseError  string   // TOML parse error message, if any
   487Ôćĺ	UnknownKeys []string // Keys in the file that don't map to any known config field
   488Ôćĺ}
   489Ôćĺ
   490Ôćĺ// ValidateConfigFile checks a config file for validity and schema conformance.
   491Ôćĺ// Returns a result indicating whether the file exists, is valid TOML, and
   492Ôćĺ// whether it contains any unknown sections or properties.
   493Ôćĺfunc ValidateConfigFile(path string) ConfigValidationResult {
   494Ôćĺ	result := ConfigValidationResult{Path: path}
   495Ôćĺ
   496Ôćĺ	// Check if file exists
   497Ôćĺ	if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   498Ôćĺ		return result
   499Ôćĺ	}
   500Ôćĺ	result.Exists = true
   501Ôćĺ
   502Ôćĺ	// Try to decode and check for unknown keys
   503Ôćĺ	var cfg Config
   504Ôćĺ	md, err := toml.DecodeFile(path, &cfg)
   505Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   506Ôćĺ		result.ParseError = err.Error()
   507Ôćĺ		return result
   508Ôćĺ	}
   509Ôćĺ	result.ValidTOML = true
   510Ôćĺ
   511Ôćĺ	// Check for keys in the TOML that didn't map to any struct field.
   512Ôćĺ	// Filter out child keys when the parent section is already unknown
   513Ôćĺ	// (e.g., if [bogus] is unknown, don't also report bogus.foo).
   514Ôćĺ	undecoded := md.Undecoded()
   515Ôćĺ	unknownSections := make(map[string]bool)
   516Ôćĺ	for _, key := range undecoded {
   517Ôćĺ		if len(key) == 1 {
   518Ôćĺ			unknownSections[key[0]] = true
   519Ôćĺ		}
   520Ôćĺ	}
   521Ôćĺ	for _, key := range undecoded {
   522Ôćĺ		// Skip child keys whose parent section is already flagged
   523Ôćĺ		if len(key) > 1 && unknownSections[key[0]] {
   524Ôćĺ			continue
   525Ôćĺ		}
   526Ôćĺ		result.UnknownKeys = append(result.UnknownKeys, key.String())
   527Ôćĺ	}
   528Ôćĺ
   529Ôćĺ	return result
   530Ôćĺ}
   531Ôćĺ
   532Ôćĺ// applyCLIOverrides applies CLI flag overrides to the config.
   533Ôćĺfunc applyCLIOverrides(cfg *Config, o *CLIOverrides) {
   534Ôćĺ	// Local sync
   535Ôćĺ	if o.OutputDir != "" {
   536Ôćĺ		cfg.LocalSync.OutputDir = o.OutputDir
   537Ôćĺ	}
   538Ôćĺ	if o.LocalTimeZone {
   539Ôćĺ		enabled := true
   540Ôćĺ		cfg.LocalSync.LocalTimeZone = &enabled
   541Ôćĺ	}
   542Ôćĺ	if o.DebugDir != "" {
   543Ôćĺ		cfg.Logging.DebugDir = o.DebugDir
   544Ôćĺ	}
   545Ôćĺ
   546Ôćĺ	// Version check (--no-version-check sets enabled to false)
   547Ôćĺ	if o.NoVersionCheck {
   548Ôćĺ		disabled := false
   549Ôćĺ		cfg.VersionCheck.Enabled = &disabled
   550Ôćĺ	}
   551Ôćĺ
   552Ôćĺ	// Cloud sync (--no-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
   553Ôćĺ	if o.NoCloudSync {
   554Ôćĺ		disabled := false
   555Ôćĺ		cfg.CloudSync.Enabled = &disabled
   556Ôćĺ	}
   557Ôćĺ
   558Ôćĺ	// Local sync (--only-cloud-sync sets enabled to false)
   559Ôćĺ	if o.OnlyCloudSync {
   560Ôćĺ		disabled := false
   561Ôćĺ		cfg.LocalSync.Enabled = &disabled
   562Ôćĺ	}
   563Ôćĺ
   564Ôćĺ	// Logging flags only override if explicitly set (true)
   565Ôćĺ	if o.Console {
   566Ôćĺ		enabled := true
   567Ôćĺ		cfg.Logging.Console = &enabled
   568Ôćĺ	}
   569Ôćĺ	if o.Log {
   570Ôćĺ		enabled := true
   571Ôćĺ		cfg.Logging.Log = &enabled
   572Ôćĺ	}
   573Ôćĺ	if o.Debug {
   574Ôćĺ		enabled := true
   575Ôćĺ		cfg.Logging.Debug = &enabled
   576Ôćĺ	}
   577Ôćĺ	if o.Silent {
   578Ôćĺ		enabled := true
   579Ôćĺ		cfg.Logging.Silent = &enabled
   580Ôćĺ	}
   581Ôćĺ
   582Ôćĺ	// Analytics (--no-usage-analytics sets enabled to false)
   583Ôćĺ	if o.NoAnalytics {
   584Ôćĺ		disabled := false
   585Ôćĺ		cfg.Analytics.Enabled = &disabled
   586Ôćĺ	}
   587Ôćĺ
   588Ôćĺ	// Telemetry
   589Ôćĺ	if o.TelemetryEndpoint != "" {
   590Ôćĺ		cfg.Telemetry.Endpoint = o.TelemetryEndpoint
   591Ôćĺ	}
   592Ôćĺ	if o.TelemetryServiceName != "" {
   593Ôćĺ		cfg.Telemetry.ServiceName = o.TelemetryServiceName
   594Ôćĺ	}
   595Ôćĺ	if o.NoTelemetryPrompts {
   596Ôćĺ		disabled := false
   597Ôćĺ		cfg.Telemetry.Prompts = &disabled
   598Ôćĺ	}
   599Ôćĺ}
   600Ôćĺ
   601Ôćĺ// --- Getter methods ---
   602Ôćĺ
   603Ôćĺ// GetOutputDir returns the output directory, or empty string to use default.
   604Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) GetOutputDir() string {
   605Ôćĺ	return c.LocalSync.OutputDir
   606Ôćĺ}
   607Ôćĺ
   608Ôćĺ// IsVersionCheckEnabled returns whether version check is enabled.
   609Ôćĺ// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
   610Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsVersionCheckEnabled() bool {
   611Ôćĺ	if c.VersionCheck.Enabled != nil {
   612Ôćĺ		return *c.VersionCheck.Enabled
   613Ôćĺ	}
   614Ôćĺ	return true // default enabled
   615Ôćĺ}
   616Ôćĺ
   617Ôćĺ// IsCloudSyncEnabled returns whether cloud sync is enabled.
   618Ôćĺ// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
   619Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsCloudSyncEnabled() bool {
   620Ôćĺ	if c.CloudSync.Enabled != nil {
   621Ôćĺ		return *c.CloudSync.Enabled
   622Ôćĺ	}
   623Ôćĺ	return true // default enabled
   624Ôćĺ}
   625Ôćĺ
   626Ôćĺ// IsLocalSyncEnabled returns whether local sync is enabled.
   627Ôćĺ// Defaults to true if not explicitly set.
   628Ôćĺ// When false, only cloud sync is performed (equivalent to --only-cloud-sync).
   629Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsLocalSyncEnabled() bool {
   630Ôćĺ	if c.LocalSync.Enabled != nil {
   631Ôćĺ		return *c.LocalSync.Enabled
   632Ôćĺ	}
   633Ôćĺ	return true // default enabled
   634Ôćĺ}
   635Ôćĺ
   636Ôćĺ// IsConsoleEnabled returns whether console logging is enabled.
   637Ôćĺ// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
   638Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsConsoleEnabled() bool {
   639Ôćĺ	if c.Logging.Console != nil {
   640Ôćĺ		return *c.Logging.Console
   641Ôćĺ	}
   642Ôćĺ	return false // default disabled
   643Ôćĺ}
   644Ôćĺ
   645Ôćĺ// IsLogEnabled returns whether file logging is enabled.
   646Ôćĺ// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
   647Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsLogEnabled() bool {
   648Ôćĺ	if c.Logging.Log != nil {
   649Ôćĺ		return *c.Logging.Log
   650Ôćĺ	}
   651Ôćĺ	return false // default disabled
   652Ôćĺ}
   653Ôćĺ
   654Ôćĺ// IsDebugEnabled returns whether debug logging is enabled.
   655Ôćĺ// Defaults to false if not explicitly set.
   656Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) IsDebugEnabled() bool {
   657Ôćĺ	if c.Logging.Debug != nil {
   658Ôćĺ		return *c.Logging.Debug
   659Ôćĺ	}
   660Ôćĺ	return false // default disabled
   661Ôćĺ}
   662Ôćĺ
   663Ôćĺ// IsSilentEnabled returns whether silent mode is enabled.

   727Ôćĺfunc (c *Config) GetProviderCmd(providerID string) string {

    19Ôćĺ// ValidateSessionData runs schema validation on SessionData when in debug mode.
    20Ôćĺ// Validation is only performed when debugRaw is true to avoid overhead in normal operation.
    21Ôćĺ// Returns true if validation passed or was skipped, false if validation failed.
    22Ôćĺfunc ValidateSessionData(session *spi.AgentChatSession, debugRaw bool) bool {
    23Ôćĺ	if !debugRaw || session.SessionData == nil {
    24Ôćĺ		return true
    25Ôćĺ	}
    26Ôćĺ	if !session.SessionData.Validate() {
    27Ôćĺ		slog.Warn("SessionData failed schema validation, proceeding anyway",
    28Ôćĺ			"sessionId", session.SessionID)
    29Ôćĺ		return false
    30Ôćĺ	}
    31Ôćĺ	return true
    32Ôćĺ}
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ// WriteDebugSessionData writes debug session data when debugRaw is enabled.
    35Ôćĺ// Logs warnings on failure but does not fail the operation.
    36Ôćĺfunc WriteDebugSessionData(session *spi.AgentChatSession, debugRaw bool) {
    37Ôćĺ	if !debugRaw || session.SessionData == nil {
    38Ôćĺ		return
    39Ôćĺ	}
    40Ôćĺ	if err := spi.WriteDebugSessionData(session.SessionID, session.SessionData); err != nil {
    41Ôćĺ		slog.Warn("Failed to write debug session data", "sessionId", session.SessionID, "error", err)
    42Ôćĺ	}
    43Ôćĺ}
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺ// FormatFilenameTimestamp formats a timestamp for use in filenames.
    46Ôćĺ// The format is filesystem-safe and matches the markdown title format.
    47Ôćĺfunc FormatFilenameTimestamp(t time.Time, useUTC bool) string {
    48Ôćĺ	if useUTC {
    49Ôćĺ		// Use UTC with Z suffix: "2006-01-02_15-04-05Z"
    50Ôćĺ		return t.UTC().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05") + "Z"
    51Ôćĺ	}
    52Ôćĺ	// Use local timezone with offset: "2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700"
    53Ôćĺ	return t.Local().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700")
    54Ôćĺ}
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ// BuildSessionFilePath constructs the full markdown file path for a session.
    57Ôćĺ// All callers need consistent filename generation from session metadata.
    58Ôćĺfunc BuildSessionFilePath(session *spi.AgentChatSession, historyDir string, useUTC bool) string {
    59Ôćĺ	timestamp, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.CreatedAt)
    60Ôćĺ	timestampStr := FormatFilenameTimestamp(timestamp, useUTC)
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ	filename := timestampStr
    63Ôćĺ	if session.Slug != "" {
    64Ôćĺ		filename = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", timestampStr, session.Slug)
    65Ôćĺ	}
    66Ôćĺ	return filepath.Join(historyDir, filename+".md")
    67Ôćĺ}
