   396Ôćĺ	}
   397Ôćĺ
   398Ôćĺ	toolTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.tool",
   399Ôćĺ		metric.WithDescription("Total tool-related tokens across all sessions (Gemini)"),
   400Ôćĺ		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
   401Ôćĺ	)
   402Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   403Ôćĺ		return err
   404Ôćĺ	}
   405Ôćĺ
   406Ôćĺ	// Droid CLI specific counters
   407Ôćĺ	thinkingTokens, err = meter.Int64Counter("specstory.tokens.thinking",
   408Ôćĺ		metric.WithDescription("Total thinking/reasoning tokens across all sessions (Droid)"),
   409Ôćĺ		metric.WithUnit("{token}"),
   410Ôćĺ	)
   411Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   412Ôćĺ		return err
   413Ôćĺ	}
   414Ôćĺ
   415Ôćĺ	return nil
   416Ôćĺ}
   417Ôćĺ
   418Ôćĺ// Shutdown flushes pending spans/metrics and shuts down both providers.
   419Ôćĺ// Safe to call even when Init was never called or telemetry is disabled.
   420Ôćĺ// For short-lived CLIs, this performs explicit ForceFlush calls before shutdown
   421Ôćĺ// to ensure all data is exported before the process exits.
   422Ôćĺfunc Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {
   423Ôćĺ	var errs []error
   424Ôćĺ
   425Ôćĺ	if traceProvider != nil {
   426Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Info("Flushing and shutting down trace provider")
   427Ôćĺ		// ForceFlush ensures all pending spans are exported before shutdown.
   428Ôćĺ		// This is critical for short-lived CLIs that may exit before the
   429Ôćĺ		// periodic exporter has time to run.
   430Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   431Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to flush trace provider", "error", err)
   432Ôćĺ		}
   433Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
   434Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("shutdown trace provider: %w", err))
   435Ôćĺ		}
   436Ôćĺ	}
   437Ôćĺ
   438Ôćĺ	if meterProvider != nil {
   439Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Info("Flushing and shutting down meter provider")
   440Ôćĺ		// ForceFlush ensures all pending metrics are exported before shutdown.
   441Ôćĺ		// The periodic reader has a 10-second interval, so without this,
   442Ôćĺ		// metrics from short CLI runs would never be sent.
   443Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   444Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to flush meter provider", "error", err)
   445Ôćĺ		}
   446Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
   447Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("shutdown meter provider: %w", err))
   448Ôćĺ		}
   449Ôćĺ	}
   450Ôćĺ
   451Ôćĺ	if len(errs) > 0 {
   452Ôćĺ		return errs[0] // Return first error
   453Ôćĺ	}
   454Ôćĺ	return nil
   455Ôćĺ}
   456Ôćĺ
   457Ôćĺ// ForceFlush explicitly flushes all pending spans and metrics.
   458Ôćĺ// Call this at the end of short-lived commands to ensure data is exported
   459Ôćĺ// before the command returns. Safe to call when telemetry is disabled.
   460Ôćĺfunc ForceFlush(ctx context.Context) error {
   461Ôćĺ	var errs []error
   462Ôćĺ
   463Ôćĺ	if traceProvider != nil {
   464Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Debug("Force flushing trace provider")
   465Ôćĺ		if err := traceProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   466Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to force flush trace provider", "error", err)
   467Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, err)
   468Ôćĺ		}
   469Ôćĺ	}
   470Ôćĺ
   471Ôćĺ	if meterProvider != nil {
   472Ôćĺ		telemetryLogger().Debug("Force flushing meter provider")
   473Ôćĺ		if err := meterProvider.ForceFlush(ctx); err != nil {
   474Ôćĺ			telemetryLogger().Warn("Failed to force flush meter provider", "error", err)
   475Ôćĺ			errs = append(errs, err)
   476Ôćĺ		}
   477Ôćĺ	}
   478Ôćĺ
   479Ôćĺ	if len(errs) > 0 {
   480Ôćĺ		return errs[0]
   481Ôćĺ	}
   482Ôćĺ	return nil
   483Ôćĺ}
   484Ôćĺ
   485Ôćĺ// Tracer returns a named tracer from the global provider. When telemetry is
   486Ôćĺ// disabled the returned tracer is a no-op (standard OTel behaviour).
   487Ôćĺfunc Tracer(name string) trace.Tracer {
   488Ôćĺ	return otel.Tracer(name)
   489Ôćĺ}
   490Ôćĺ
   491Ôćĺ// Meter returns the meter instance. When telemetry is disabled, returns the
   492Ôćĺ// global no-op meter.
   493Ôćĺfunc Meter() metric.Meter {
   494Ôćĺ	if meter != nil {
   495Ôćĺ		return meter
   496Ôćĺ	}
   497Ôćĺ	return otel.Meter("specstory-cli")
   498Ôćĺ}
   499Ôćĺ
   500Ôćĺ// ContextWithSessionTrace returns a context that will cause any spans started
   501Ôćĺ// from it to share the same trace ID (derived deterministically from sessionID).
   502Ôćĺ// This groups all spans for a given session into a single trace, even across
   503Ôćĺ// multiple invocations (e.g., in autosave mode where processSingleSession is
   504Ôćĺ// called repeatedly as the session file grows).
   505Ôćĺfunc ContextWithSessionTrace(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) context.Context {
   506Ôćĺ	traceID := traceIDFromSessionID(sessionID)
   507Ôćĺ	spanID := spanIDFromSessionID(sessionID)
   508Ôćĺ
   509Ôćĺ	// Create a SpanContext with both deterministic trace ID and span ID.
   510Ôćĺ	// In OpenTelemetry-Go, a SpanContext is only valid if both TraceID and SpanID
   511Ôćĺ	// are valid. We mark it as Remote so child spans inherit the trace ID.
   512Ôćĺ	sc := trace.NewSpanContext(trace.SpanContextConfig{
   513Ôćĺ		TraceID:    traceID,
   514Ôćĺ		SpanID:     spanID,
   515Ôćĺ		TraceFlags: trace.FlagsSampled, // Ensure it's sampled
   516Ôćĺ		Remote:     true,               // Mark as remote parent
   517Ôćĺ	})
   518Ôćĺ
   519Ôćĺ	// Inject the SpanContext as a remote parent so child spans inherit the trace ID
   520Ôćĺ	return trace.ContextWithRemoteSpanContext(ctx, sc)
   521Ôćĺ}
   522Ôćĺ
   523Ôćĺ// traceIDFromSessionID generates a deterministic 16-byte trace ID from a session ID
   524Ôćĺ// by hashing the session ID with SHA-256 and taking the first 16 bytes.
   525Ôćĺfunc traceIDFromSessionID(sessionID string) trace.TraceID {
   526Ôćĺ	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(sessionID))
   527Ôćĺ	var traceID trace.TraceID
   528Ôćĺ	copy(traceID[:], hash[:16])
   529Ôćĺ	return traceID
   530Ôćĺ}
   531Ôćĺ
   532Ôćĺ// spanIDFromSessionID generates a deterministic 8-byte span ID from a session ID
   533Ôćĺ// by hashing the session ID with SHA-256 and taking bytes 16-24.
   534Ôćĺ// This ensures the span ID is different from the trace ID but still deterministic.
   535Ôćĺfunc spanIDFromSessionID(sessionID string) trace.SpanID {
   536Ôćĺ	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(sessionID))
   537Ôćĺ	var spanID trace.SpanID
   538Ôćĺ	copy(spanID[:], hash[16:24])
   539Ôćĺ	return spanID
   540Ôćĺ}
   541Ôćĺ
   542Ôćĺ// --- Metric Recording Functions ---
   543Ôćĺ// These are internal functions called by RecordSessionMetrics in session_helpers.go.
   544Ôćĺ// The metricsEnabled check is done once in RecordSessionMetrics.
   545Ôćĺ
   546Ôćĺ// buildMetricAttrs combines specific metric attributes with common attributes from OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES.
   547Ôćĺfunc buildMetricAttrs(specific ...attribute.KeyValue) []attribute.KeyValue {
   548Ôćĺ	attrs := make([]attribute.KeyValue, 0, len(specific)+len(commonMetricAttrs))
   549Ôćĺ	attrs = append(attrs, specific...)
   550Ôćĺ	attrs = append(attrs, commonMetricAttrs...)
   551Ôćĺ	return attrs
   552Ôćĺ}
   553Ôćĺ
   554Ôćĺ// recordSessionProcessed increments the sessions processed counter.
   555Ôćĺfunc recordSessionProcessed(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string) {
   556Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   557Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   558Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   559Ôćĺ	)
   560Ôćĺ	sessionsProcessed.Add(ctx, 1, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   561Ôćĺ}
   562Ôćĺ
   563Ôćĺ// recordExchanges increments the exchanges counter by the given count.
   564Ôćĺfunc recordExchanges(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   565Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   566Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   567Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   568Ôćĺ	)
   569Ôćĺ	exchangesProcessed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   570Ôćĺ}
   571Ôćĺ
   572Ôćĺ// recordMessages increments the messages counter by the given count.
   573Ôćĺfunc recordMessages(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   574Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   575Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   576Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   577Ôćĺ	)
   578Ôćĺ	messagesProcessed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   579Ôćĺ}
   580Ôćĺ
   581Ôćĺ// recordToolUsage increments the tool usage counter by the given count.
   582Ôćĺfunc recordToolUsage(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, count int64) {
   583Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   584Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   585Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   586Ôćĺ	)
   587Ôćĺ	toolsUsed.Add(ctx, count, metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   588Ôćĺ}
   589Ôćĺ
   590Ôćĺ// recordProcessingDuration records the session processing duration.
   591Ôćĺfunc recordProcessingDuration(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, duration time.Duration) {
   592Ôćĺ	attrs := buildMetricAttrs(
   593Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.agent", agent),
   594Ôćĺ		attribute.String("specstory.session.id", sessionID),
   595Ôćĺ	)
   596Ôćĺ	processingDuration.Record(ctx, duration.Seconds(), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   597Ôćĺ}
   598Ôćĺ
   599Ôćĺ// TokenUsage holds aggregated token counts for telemetry recording.
   600Ôćĺ// Different providers track different token types:
   601Ôćĺ//   - Claude Code: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CacheCreationInputTokens, CacheReadInputTokens
   602Ôćĺ//   - Codex CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedInputTokens, ReasoningOutputTokens
   603Ôćĺ//   - Gemini CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CachedTokens, ThoughtTokens, ToolTokens
   604Ôćĺ//   - Droid CLI: InputTokens, OutputTokens, CacheCreationInputTokens, CacheReadInputTokens, ThinkingTokens
   605Ôćĺtype TokenUsage struct {
   606Ôćĺ	// Common fields (all providers)
   607Ôćĺ	InputTokens  int
   608Ôćĺ	OutputTokens int
   609Ôćĺ
   610Ôćĺ	// Claude Code specific (also used by Droid CLI)
   611Ôćĺ	CacheCreationInputTokens int
   612Ôćĺ	CacheReadInputTokens     int
   613Ôćĺ
   614Ôćĺ	// Codex CLI specific
   615Ôćĺ	CachedInputTokens     int
   616Ôćĺ	ReasoningOutputTokens int
   617Ôćĺ
   618Ôćĺ	// Gemini CLI specific
   619Ôćĺ	CachedTokens  int
   620Ôćĺ	ThoughtTokens int
   621Ôćĺ	ToolTokens    int
   622Ôćĺ
   623Ôćĺ	// Droid CLI specific
   624Ôćĺ	ThinkingTokens int
   625Ôćĺ}
   626Ôćĺ
   627Ôćĺ// recordTokenUsage records token usage metrics for a session.

   629Ôćĺfunc recordTokenUsage(ctx context.Context, agent string, sessionID string, usage TokenUsage) {

   635Ôćĺ	// Common metrics

   651Ôćĺ	// Codex CLI specific

   653Ôćĺ		cachedInputTokens.Add(ctx, int64(usage.CachedInputTokens), metric.WithAttributes(attrs...))
   654Ôćĺ	}
   655Ôćĺ	if usage.ReasoningOutputTokens > 0 {

Now I have the full file. Here are the two changes I'll make:

**Observation 30** ÔÇö `telemetryLogger()` creates a new `slog.Logger` with `.With("component", "telemetry")` on every call. Since the base logger never changes after process startup, I'll cache it in a package-level `var` initialized via `sync.Once`.

**Observation 32** ÔÇö `defaultEndpoint` is only used in the `raw == ""` branch of `parseEndpoint`, but `Init` is never called with an empty endpoint (the caller checks `IsTelemetryEnabled()` which requires a non-empty endpoint). I'll remove the `defaultEndpoint` constant, the unreachable empty-string branch, and update the doc comment on `Options.Endpoint` and `parseEndpoint`.

   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ// parseEndpoint normalises an OTLP endpoint string into a bare host:port suitable
   120Ôćĺ// for otlptracegrpc.WithEndpoint and a flag indicating whether TLS should be disabled.
   121Ôćĺ//
   122Ôćĺ// Accepted formats:
   123Ôćĺ//
   124Ôćĺ//	"localhost:4317"                 Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",          insecure=true
   125Ôćĺ//	"http://localhost:4317"          Ôćĺ host="localhost:4317",          insecure=true
   126Ôćĺ//	"https://collector.example:4317" Ôćĺ host="collector.example:4317",  insecure=false
   127Ôćĺfunc parseEndpoint(raw string) (host string, insecure bool) {
   128Ôćĺ	u, err := url.Parse(raw)
   129Ôćĺ	if err != nil || u.Host == "" {
   130Ôćĺ		// Not a URL ÔÇö treat the whole string as host:port (bare address).
   131Ôćĺ		return raw, true
   132Ôćĺ	}
   133Ôćĺ
   134Ôćĺ	// Parsed successfully as a URL with a scheme.
   135Ôćĺ	host = u.Host
   136Ôćĺ	insecure = u.Scheme != "https"
   137Ôćĺ	return host, insecure
   138Ôćĺ}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ// Init configures the OTel tracing and metrics subsystem. Thread-safe and idempotent ÔÇö only the
   141Ôćĺ// first call takes effect. When Enabled is false the global no-op provider remains
   142Ôćĺ// active, so callers can emit spans/events and record metrics safely with zero overhead.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-23 22:47:45Z)**_

Both changes are clean. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Observation 30 ÔÇö Cache `telemetryLogger()`**: Replaced the function that allocated a new `slog.Logger` on every call with a `sync.Once`-guarded package-level `logger` variable. The logger is created once on first use and reused thereafter.

**Observation 32 ÔÇö Remove unreachable `defaultEndpoint` fallback**: 
- Removed the `defaultEndpoint` constant (it was only referenced in dead code)
- Removed the `if raw == ""` branch in `parseEndpoint` ÔÇö this was unreachable because `IsTelemetryEnabled()` requires a non-empty endpoint, so `Init` never calls `parseEndpoint("")`
- Updated the `Options.Endpoint` doc comment to say "must be non-empty when Enabled is true" instead of "default localhost:4317"
- Removed the `""` format line from the `parseEndpoint` godoc

Build, lint (0 issues), and all telemetry tests pass.
